## PAGE 00132: (2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0

- page_id: `272740574`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740574/2026x+Installing+FlexNet+11.17.0+11.18.2+11.19.0
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:21.167+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing > (2026x) FlexNet license server 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

#### EXCERPT: For OS X and Linux users

INLINE

##### Installation procedure

###### For Windows

1. Install the FlexNet license server manager. We highly recommend running the FlexNet license server (*lmadmin*) as a service (on Windows).As a result, the server can start automatically after the machine boots. Starting the server manually is not user-friendly – there is no GUI for this action.If your operating system is Windows, you only need to select the **Run as a service** check box when prompted during the *lmadmin* installation.[IMAGE alt='' src='']For the instructions on how to create a daemon, refer to Automatic Start in:[ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_17_0.pdf'][ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_18_2.pdf'][ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.19.0.pdf']
2. Place the vendor daemon - Cameo into the FlexNet server install folder. If you use *lmadmin*, do the following: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Note that all files in *install* and *data (for Windows)* dirs must have read/write permissions. *lmadmin* or cameo daemon may not start even if a log file or cameo daemon does not have r/w permissions.Use this command to set permissions for all files within the directory recursively.chmod -R 755 FNPLicenseServerManager
  1. Open the FlexNet server install folder.
  2. In the install folder, create the licenses\cameo folder.
  3. Place the Vendor daemon in *<FlexNet server data folder>\licenses\cameo*.
3. Start the server (as a service), or using the command line manually by running the *lmadmin* file from the FlexNet server installation directory. Running lmadmin using command line on WindowsIf a service was not installed and you want to run your lmadmin using command line, you will have to define the additional required parameters. e.g. -configDir. The dir is located in your *data* dir (for example *C:\ProgramData\FLEXlm\lmadmin or any other location you defined while installing).*

###### For Linux

1. Install the FlexNet license server manager. We highly recommend running the FlexNet license server (*lmadmin*) as a daemon (on OS X or Linux).As a result, the server can start automatically after the machine boots. Starting the server manually is not user-friendly – there is no GUI for this action.For the instructions on how to create a daemon, refer to Automatic Start in:[ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_17_0.pdf'][ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_18_2.pdf'][ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.19.0.pdf']
2. Place the vendor daemon - Cameo into the FlexNet server install folder. If you use *lmadmin*, do the following:
  1. Open the FlexNet server install folder. For OS X and Linux usersIf you don't have permission to open the FlexNet server data folder, execute the following command from the root:On OS X 
chmod g+x FNPLicenseServerManager/ 
chmod –R g+w ./*On Linuxchmod g+x FNPLicenseServerManager/
  2. In the install folder, create the licenses\cameo folder.
  3. Place the Vendor daemon in *<FlexNet server data folder>\licenses\cameo*.
3. Start the server (as a service), or using the command line manually by running the *lmadmin* file from the FlexNet server installation directory.

If you come across any issues while installing/running the software, please refer to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Troubleshooting' space=''].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740567 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00133: (2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.19.6, 11.19.8

- page_id: `272740567`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740567/2026x+Installing+FlexNet+11.19.6+11.19.8
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:20.856+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing > (2026x) FlexNet license server 11.19.6, 11.19.8
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

#### EXCERPT: For OS X and Linux users

INLINE

##### Installation procedure

###### For Windows

1. Install the FlexNet license server manager.
2. When installing, choose the FlexNet server *<install folder>*and the *<data folder>*. You can use the same directory for both. We highly recommend running the FlexNet license server (*lmadmin*) as a service (on Windows).As a result, the server can start automatically after the machine boots. Starting the server manually is not user-friendly – there is no GUI for this action.If your operating system is Windows, you only need to select the **Run as a service** check box when prompted during the *lmadmin* installation.[IMAGE alt='' src='']For the instructions on how to create a daemon, refer to Automatic Start in:[ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_19_6.pdf'][ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.19.8.pdf']
3. Place the vendor daemon - cameo, into the previously chosen *<FlexNet server data folder>*. If you use *lmadmin*, do the following: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Note that all files in *install* and *data (for Windows)* dirs must have read/write permissions. *lmadmin* or cameo daemon may not start even if a log file or cameo daemon does not have r/w permissions.
  1. Open the *<FlexNet server data folder>* (by default ProgramData\FLEXlm\lmadmin).
  2. In the data folder, create the licenses\cameo folder.
  3. Place the Vendor daemon in *<FlexNet server data folder>\licenses\cameo*.
4. Start the server (as a service), or using the command line manually by running the *lmadmin* file from the FlexNet server installation directory. Running lmadmin using command line on WindowsIf a service was not installed and you want to run your lmadmin using command line, you will have to define the additional required parameters. e.g. -configDir. The dir is located in your *data* dir (for example *C:\ProgramData\FLEXlm\lmadmin or any other location you defined while installing).*

###### For Linux

1. Install the FlexNet license server manager. We highly recommend running the FlexNet license server (*lmadmin*) as a daemon (on OS X or Linux).As a result, the server can start automatically after the machine boots. Starting the server manually is not user-friendly – there is no GUI for this action.For the instructions on how to create a daemon, refer to Automatic Start in:[ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_19_6.pdf'][ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.19.8.pdf']
2. Place the vendor daemon - cameo, into the *<FlexNet server install folder>*. If you use *lmadmin*, do the following:
  1. Open the FlexNet server install folder. For OS X and Linux usersIf you don't have permission to open the FlexNet server data folder, execute the following command from the root:On OS X 
chmod g+x FNPLicenseServerManager/ 
chmod –R g+w ./*On Linuxchmod g+x FNPLicenseServerManager/
  2. In the <FlexNet server install folder> , create the licenses\cameo folder.
  3. Place the Vendor daemon in *<FlexNet server install folder>\licenses\cameo*.
3. Start the server (as a service), or using the command line manually by running the *lmadmin* file from the FlexNet server installation directory.

If you come across any issues while installing/running the software, please refer to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Troubleshooting' space=''].

If you use <em>lmadmin</em>, do the following:</p><ol><li><p>Open the FlexNet server install folder.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9817fdfc-9b3f-4451-bbd9-f011b853aee8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">For OS X and Linux users</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you don't have permission to open the FlexNet server data folder, execute the following command from the root:</p><ul><li>On OS X<br /><pre>chmod g+x FNPLicenseServerManager/<br />chmod –R g+w ./*</pre></li></ul><p><br /></p><ul><li><p>On Linux</p><pre>chmod g+x FNPLicenseServerManager/</pre></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the <em>&lt;FlexNet server install folder&gt;</em>, create the <em>licenses\cameo </em>folder.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Place the Vendor daemon in <em>&lt;FlexNet server install folder&gt;\licenses\cameo</em>.</p></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="3d334102-03d6-4ef4-9ded-60db0b409d66"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Start the server (as a service), or using the command line manually by running the <em>lmadmin</em> file from the FlexNet server installation directory.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>If you come across any issues while installing/running the software, please refer to<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Troubleshooting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Troubleshooting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00134: (2026x) Installing FlexNet license server 11.12, 11.14, 11.16

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: For OS X and Linux users

**On this page**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: For OS X and Linux users

1081226172

#### EXCERPT: For OS X and Linux users

INLINE

##### Installation procedure

###### For Windows

1. Install the FlexNet license server manager. We highly recommend running the FlexNet license server (*lmadmin*) as a service (on Windows).As a result, the server can start automatically after the machine boots. Starting the server manually is not user-friendly – there is no GUI for this action.If your operating system is Windows, you only need to select the **Run as a service** check box when prompted during the *lmadmin* installation.[IMAGE alt='' src='']For the instructions on how to create a daemon, refer to Automatic Start in:[ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_12_1.pdf'][ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.16.4.pdf']
2. Place the vendor daemon - Cameo into the FlexNet server install folder. If you use *lmadmin*, do the following:
  1. Open the FlexNet server installation folder.
  2. In the open folder, create the licenses\cameo folder.
  3. Place the vendor daemon in *<FlexNet server installation folder>\licenses\cameo*. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Note that all files in FNPLicenseServerManager must have read/write permissions. *lmadmin* or cameo daemon may not start even if a log file or cameo daemon does not have r/w permissions.Use this command to set permissions for all files within the directory recursively.chmod -R 755 FNPLicenseServerManager
3. Start the server by running the *lmadmin* file from the FlexNet server installation directory.

###### For Linux

1. Install the FlexNet license server manager. We highly recommend running the FlexNet license server (*lmadmin*) as a daemon (on OS X or Linux).As a result, the server can start automatically after the machine boots. Starting the server manually is not user-friendly – there is no GUI for this action.For the instructions on how to create a daemon, refer to Automatic Start in:[ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_12_1.pdf'][ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.16.4.pdf']
2. Place the vendor daemon - Cameo into the FlexNet server install folder. If you use *lmadmin*, do the following:
  1. Open the FlexNet server installation folder. For OS X and Linux usersIf you don't have permission to open the FlexNet server installation folder, execute the following command from the root:On OS X 
chmod g+x FNPLicenseServerManager/ 
chmod –R g+w ./*On Linuxchmod g+x FNPLicenseServerManager/
  2. In the open folder, create the licenses\cameo folder.
  3. Place the vendor daemon in *<FlexNet server installation folder>\licenses\cameo*.
3. Start the server by running the lmadmin file from the FlexNet server installation directory.

If you come across any issues while installing/running the software, please refer to[Troubleshooting](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/NMDOC/Troubleshooting).

## PAGE 00135: (2026x) Installing modeling tools

- page_id: `272740398`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740398/2026x+Installing+modeling+tools
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:12.377+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins installation > (2026x) Installing modeling tools and plugins
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: Note

INLINE

##### Prerequisites

- Ensure your machine meets the minimum [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) System requirements' space=''] for running the modeling tool.
- An appropriate [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Java version support' space=''] should be installed on your machine (optional).
- **IMPORTANT for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DSLS installation and licensing' space='']**. Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable must be installed (see Latest supported Visual C++ Redistributable downloads ). This applies to Windows OS only.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading installation files' space='']

##### Installation workflow

1. Install the modeling tool following the installation instructions according to your operating system. Demo Version filesStarting with19.0 SP2, Demo version comes as a separate install file anddoes not requirethe license key.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding the license key' space=''] the license key or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool' space='']the floating license.

#### WARNING: For the users of Academic license

For the users of Academic license

By default, the licensing information is stored in each user's home directory. If you are using the Academic license in class, we recommend saving the configuration files (which include the licensing information) in the modeling tool installation directory. The applied academic license will be available for all users of the particular machine when the configuration files are stored in the recommended location. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='MT'] for instructions on changing the modeling tool configuration file location.

You must install the desktop software on your computer to use the modeling tool.The installation procedures provided below are for the standard installation as well as the advanced. However, it is strongly recommended to use the standard installation procedure.

##### Installing on Windows

###### Standard installation

1. Double-click the installer.
2. If the User Account Control dialog appears asking permission for the installer to make changes in your computer, click Yes .
3. Follow the on-screen instructions.
4. Start the modeling tool by doing one of the following: ImportantWe recommend starting the modeling tool as an administrator. Right-click the *<modeling tool>.exe* file. Select **Run as administrator** from the shortcut menu.
  - On the taskbar in Windows, click the Start button, and then, click the modeling tool (for example, MagicDraw) .
  - Open the *<modeling tool> installation*\*bin* folder and double-click the modeling tool .exe file (for example, m*agicdraw.exe)*.
5. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='MT'] dialog, do one of the following:
  - If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool version, select the location and click Import .
  - If you want to use the default environment options, click Use Default .
6. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding the license key' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool' space=''] the floating license.

###### Installation using no-installer

#### WARNING: Important

Important

This is an advanced procedure for installing the modeling tool. Please use this procedure only if you are familiar with the modeling tool and/or the standard installation does not support your use case.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

An appropriate[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Java version support' space='']should be installed on your computer before you begin.

1. Extract <modeling tool>_<version number> _no_install.zip .
2. Set Java home:
  1. Open the <modeling tool>.properties file, which can be found in <modeling tool>_<version number> _no_install\bin folder.
  2. In the JAVA_HOME line, add the path to the Java software. For example: JAVA_HOME=C\:\\Program Files\\Java\\jdk11.0.9
3. To start the modeling tool, open the <modeling tool>_<version number> _ no_install\bin folder and double-click the <modeling tool>.exe file.
4. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='MT'] dialog, do one of the following:
  - If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool version, select the location and click Import .
  - If you want to use the default environment options, click Use Default .
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding the license key' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool' space=''] the floating license.

##### Installing on OS X

###### Standard installation

1. Double-click the installer.
2. In the modeling tool installation window, drag the <modeling tool> folder to the Applications folder.
3. Close the modeling tool installation window.
4. Start the modeling tool. Open the Application / <modeling tool> installation folder and double-click the modeling tool .app file (for example, MagicDraw.app) .
5. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='MT'] dialog, do one of the following:
  - If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool version, select the location and click Import .
  - If you want to use the default environment options, click Use Default .
6. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding the license key' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool' space=''] the floating license.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If the message appears asking permission to open the file, click **Open**.

###### Installation using no-installer

#### WARNING: Important

Important

This is an advanced procedure for installing the modeling tool. Please use this procedure only if you are familiar with the modeling tool and/or the standard installation does not support your use case.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

An appropriate[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Java version support' space='']should be installed on your computer before you begin.

1. Extract <modeling tool>_ <version number>_no_instal.zip .
2. Set Java home:
  1. Open the <modeling tool>.properties file, which can be found in <modeling tool>_<version number> _no_install\bin folder.
  2. In the JAVA_HOME line, add the path to the Java software. For example: JAVA_HOME= /Users/<user_name>/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/jdk11.0.9.jdk/Contents/Home/
3. To start the modeling tool, open the <modeling tool>_<version number> _no_install folder and double-click the <modeling tool>.app file.
4. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='MT'] dialog, do one of the following:
  - If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool version, select the location and click Import .
  - If you want to use the default environment options, click Use Default .
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding the license key' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool' space=''] the floating license.

##### Installing on Unix

###### Standard installation

1. From the <modeling tool>_ <version number>_unix.sh shortcut menu, select the Properties command and then click to select the Allow executing file as program check box.
2. Double-click the installer.
3. Follow the on-screen instructions to install the modeling tool.
4. Start the modeling tool. Open the <modeling tool> installation/bin folder and double-click the modeling tool file (for example, magicdraw).
5. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='MT'] dialog, do one of the following:
  - If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool version, select the location and click Import .
  - If you want to use the default environment options, click Use Default .
6. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding the license key' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool' space=''] the floating license.

###### Installation using no-installer

#### WARNING: Important

Important

This is an advanced procedure for installing the modeling tool. Please use this procedure only if you are familiar with the modeling tool and/or the standard installation does not support your use case.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

An appropriate[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Java version support' space='']should be installed on your computer before you begin.

1. Extract <modeling tool>_<version number> _no_install.zip .
2. Set Java home:
  1. Open the <modeling tool> .properties file, which can be found in <modeling tool>_<version number> _no_install\bin folder.
  2. In the JAVA_HOME line, add the path to the Java software. For example: JAVA_HOME=/usr/local/java/jre11.0.9
3. To start the modeling tool, open the <modeling tool>_<version number> _ no_install/bin folder and double-click the <modeling tool> file.
4. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='MT'] dialog, do one of the following:
  - If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool version, select the location and click Import .
  - If you want to use the default environment options, click Use Default .
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding the license key' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool' space=''] the floating license.

##### Bundling JRE for multiple operating systems

If you want to configure the modeling tool for multiple operating systems, you can manually customize the *<modeling tool>.properties* file in order for the tool to automatically detect the relevant operating system upon its launch. This eliminates the need to customize the JRE path on each individual operating system where the bundled modeling tool is used.

JREs should be bundled for each operating system in different subdirectories.

1. Open the <modeling tool>.properties file, which can be found in <modeling tool>_<version number> _no_install\bin folder.
2. In the JAVA_HOME line, add the path to the Java software for a specific operating system. Duplicate the line for any additional operating systems you may be using, adding their relative paths. For example:

*JAVA_HOME_WINDOWS=jre/windows**JAVA_HOME_MACOSX=jre/macosx/mac/Contents/Home/jre**JAVA_HOME_LINUX=jre/linux*

When you are finished with the installation, the following page will provide you with more information onsetting up your modeling tool:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installing plugins' space='']

If you will be using a floating license server, all relevant information can be found here:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing' space='']

If you run into further problems with installation:

- Check the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Frequently Asked Questions' space='FAQ'] for known problems
- Check the [No Magic Community forum](https://community.nomagic.com/)
- Contact customer [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support' space='SUP'].

However, it is strongly recommended to use the standard installation procedure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span></p><h3 class="style1">Installing on Windows</h3><h4>Standard installation</h4><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the installer.</li><li>If the <strong>User Account Control </strong>dialog appears asking permission for the installer to make changes in your computer, click <strong>Yes</strong>.</li><li>Follow the on-screen instructions.</li><li><p>Start the modeling tool by doing one of the following:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8baa0977-6720-4878-8851-2301b039700d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span class="diffcontext">We recommend starting the modeling tool as an administrator. Right-click the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;.exe</em> file. Select <strong>Run as administrator</strong> from the shortcut menu.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>On the taskbar in Windows, click the <strong>Start</strong> button, and then, click the modeling tool (for example, <em>MagicDraw)</em>.</li><li><p>Open the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt; installation</em>\<em>bin</em> folder and double-click the modeling tool .exe file (for example, m<em>agicdraw.exe)</em>.</p></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the </span><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Configuration files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Import Configuration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> dialog, do one of the following:</span><ul class="style1"><li>If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool version, select the location and click <strong>Import</strong>.</li><li>If you want to use the default environment options, click <strong>Use Default</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding the license key" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add the license key]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">or </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[obtain ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the </span>floating<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> license.</span></li></ol><h4>Installation using no-installer</h4><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9abca5c7-3bc9-4b02-b929-8d9a845de5cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This is an advanced procedure for installing the modeling tool. Please use this procedure only if you are familiar with the modeling tool and/or the standard installation does not support your use case.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d125575d-a244-4089-b6ed-be2f72272839"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">An appropriate </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Java version support" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[JVM version]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> should be installed on your computer before you begin.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li>Extract <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;_&lt;version number&gt;</em><em>_no_install.zip</em>.</li><li>Set Java home:<ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;.properties</em> file, which can be found in <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;_&lt;version number&gt;</em><em>_no_install\bin</em> folder.</li><li>In the <em>JAVA_HOME</em> line, add the path to the Java software. For example:<br /><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6bb2f95d-3fae-4f9a-ab29-b1d681014b09">JAVA_HOME=C\:\\Program Files\\Java\\jdk11.0.9</ac:inline-comment-marker></em></li></ol></li><li>To start the modeling tool, open the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;_&lt;version number&gt;</em>_<em>no_install\bin</em> folder and double-click the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;.exe</em> file.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the </span><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Configuration files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Import Configuration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> dialog, do one of the following:</span><ul class="style1"><li>If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool version, select the location and click <strong>Import</strong>.</li><li>If you want to use the default environment options, click <strong>Use Default</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding the license key" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add the license key]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">or </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[obtain ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">floating</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> license.</span></li></ol><h3 class="style1">Installing on OS X</h3><h4>Standard installation</h4><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the installer.</li><li>In the modeling tool installation window, drag the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;</em> folder to the <em>Applications</em> folder.</li><li>Close the <em>modeling tool</em> installation window.</li><li>Start the modeling tool. Open the <em>Application</em> / <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt; installation</em> folder and double-click the modeling tool .app file (for example, <em>MagicDraw.app)</em>.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the </span><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Configuration files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Import Configuration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> dialog, do one of the following:</span><ul class="style1"><li>If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool version, select the location and click <strong>Import</strong>.</li><li>If you want to use the default environment options, click <strong>Use Default</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding the license key" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add the license key]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">or </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[obtain ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">floating</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> license.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01c10d62-195c-4d03-b6f5-8b0b4540773d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the message appears asking permission to open the file, click <strong>Open</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Installation using no-installer</h4><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a342638-bd1d-4bc6-90c2-5eb53b8007f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This is an advanced procedure for installing the modeling tool. Please use this procedure only if you are familiar with the modeling tool and/or the standard installation does not support your use case.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1211027b-d81e-4a2a-88ec-a745ef3c5687"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">An appropriate </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Java version support" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[JVM version]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> should be installed on your computer before you begin.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li>Extract <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;_</em><em>&lt;version number&gt;_no_instal.zip</em>.</li><li>Set Java home:<ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;.properties</em> file, which can be found in <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;_&lt;version number&gt;</em><em>_no_install\bin</em> folder.</li><li>In the <em>JAVA_HOME</em> line, add the path to the Java software. For example:<br />JAVA_HOME=<em>/Users/&lt;user_name&gt;/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/jdk11.0.9.jdk/Contents/Home/</em></li></ol></li><li>To start the modeling tool, open the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;_&lt;version number&gt;</em><em>_no_install</em> folder and double-click the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;.app</em> file.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the </span><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Configuration files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Import Configuration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> dialog, do one of the following:</span><ul class="style1"><li>If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool version, select the location and click <strong>Import</strong>.</li><li>If you want to use the default environment options, click <strong>Use Default</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding the license key" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add the license key]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">or </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[obtain ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">floating</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> license.</span></li></ol><h3>Installing on Unix</h3><h4>Standard installation</h4><hr /><ol><li>From the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;_</em><em>&lt;version number&gt;_unix.sh</em> shortcut menu, select the <strong>Properties</strong> command and then click to select the <strong>Allow executing file as program</strong> check box. </li><li>Double-click the installer.</li><li>Follow the on-screen instructions to install the modeling tool.</li><li>Start the modeling tool. Open the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt; installation/bin</em> folder and double-click the modeling tool file (for example, <em>magicdraw).</em></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the </span><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Configuration files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Import Configuration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> dialog, do one of the following:</span><ul class="style1"><li>If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool version, select the location and click <strong>Import</strong>.</li><li>If you want to use the default environment options, click <strong>Use Default</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding the license key" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add the license key]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">or </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[obtain ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">floating</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> license.</span><p><br /></p></li></ol><h4>Installation using no-installer</h4><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aca76ddc-488c-445e-a1a3-408ed68259f5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This is an advanced procedure for installing the modeling tool. Please use this procedure only if you are familiar with the modeling tool and/or the standard installation does not support your use case.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e3974a9-965c-426e-9e97-d8e4779e066c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">An appropriate </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Java version support" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[JVM version]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> should be installed on your computer before you begin.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li>Extract <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;_&lt;version number&gt;</em><em>_no_install.zip</em>.</li><li>Set Java home:<ol><li>Open the &lt;modeling tool&gt;<em>.properties</em> file, which can be found in <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;_&lt;version number&gt;</em><em>_no_install\bin</em> folder.</li><li>In the <em>JAVA_HOME</em> line, add the path to the Java software. For example:<br /><em>JAVA_HOME=/usr/local/java/jre11.0.9</em></li></ol></li><li>To start the modeling tool, open the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;_&lt;version number&gt;</em>_<em>no_install/bin </em>folder and double-click the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;</em> file.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In the </span><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Configuration files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Import Configuration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> dialog, do one of the following:</span><ul class="style1"><li>If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool version, select the location and click <strong>Import</strong>.</li><li>If you want to use the default environment options, click <strong>Use Default</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Adding the license key" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add the license key]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">or </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[obtain ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">floating</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> license.</span></li></ol><h3>Bundling JRE for multiple operating systems</h3><p>If you want to configure the modeling tool for multiple operating systems, you can manually customize the <em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;.properties</em> file in order for the tool to automatically detect the relevant operating system upon its launch. This eliminates the need to customize the JRE path on each individual operating system where the bundled modeling tool is used. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d9c320a-ea52-4e80-93db-c0d1cf64c43b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>JREs should be bundled for each operating system in different subdirectories.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li><span>Open the </span><em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;.properties</em><span> file, which can be found in </span><em>&lt;modeling tool&gt;_&lt;version number&gt;</em><em>_no_install\bin</em><span> folder.</span></li><li><span>In the </span><em>JAVA_HOME</em><span> line, add the path to the Java software for a specific operating system. Duplicate the line for any additional operating systems you may be using, adding their relative paths. For example:</span></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;"><em>JAVA_HOME_WINDOWS=jre/windows<br /></em><em>JAVA_HOME_MACOSX=jre/macosx/mac/Contents/Home/jre<br /></em><em>JAVA_HOME_LINUX=jre/linux</em></p><p><br /></p><hr /><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">When you are finished with the installation, the following page will provide you with more information on </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">setting up your modeling tool:</span></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing plugins" /></ac:link></li></ul><p>If you will be using a floating license server, all relevant information can be found here:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If you run into further problems with installation:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Check the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="Frequently Asked Questions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[FAQ section]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">for known problems</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Check the</span> <a href="https://community.nomagic.com/">No Magic Community forum</a></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contact customer <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SUP" ri:content-title="Support" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[support]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740396 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00136: (2026x) Installing modeling tools and plugins

- page_id: `272740396`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740396/2026x+Installing+modeling+tools+and+plugins
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:12.337+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins installation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The section explains how to perform the standard step-by-step installation and no-install run of Magic Software Architect, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Magic Systems of Systems Architect, MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, and Cameo Enterprise Architecture modeling tools on Windows, OSx, and Unix. Additionally, it focuses on the installation of plugins in modeling tools. You will learn how to install a plugin from the downloaded .zip archive and install a plugin manually.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68949ca0-72c0-46a0-b149-c966c61a0ec8"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The section explains how to perform the standard step-by-step installation and no-install run of Magic Software Architect, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Magic Systems of Systems Architect, MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, and Cameo Enterprise Architecture modeling tools on Windows, OSx, and Unix. Additionally, it focuses on the installation of plugins in modeling tools. You will learn how to install a plugin from the downloaded .zip archive and install a plugin manually. </span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b30777df-69af-4603-9e70-34b304466351" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740400 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00137: (2026x) Installing plugins

- page_id: `272740400`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740400/2026x+Installing+plugins
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:12.447+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins installation > (2026x) Installing modeling tools and plugins
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For information about plugin installation in versions prior to the 2022x version, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installing plugins in earlier versions' space=''] page.

#### EXCERPT: Getting help

INLINE

Choose one of the following ways to install a plugin in a modeling tool:

- 
- 
  - Adding plugins from an *.rdzip* file located on a local file system or network share
  - 
- 
  - Installing an individual plugin by importing a *.zip* file
  - Installing an individual plugin by extracting a *.zip* file

##### Installing our commercial and no-cost plugins

All available plugins provided by us, both commercial and no-cost, are accessible via the *core.rdzip* file, which is pre-bundled together with the installation files and is readily available in the **Resource/Plugin Manager**. The available plugins depend on the modeling tool that you are using. The file is located at *<tool_installation_directory>\data\resourcemanager\distribution\core.rdzip*, however, it is added automatically to the **Resource/Plugin Manager**.

Add pre-bundled plugin bundleTo install our commercial and no-cost plugins

1. In the main menu, select Help > Resource/Plugin Manager . The pre-bundled core.rdzip file containing all available plugins is loaded automatically.
2. Select the check boxes for the plugins you want to install.
3. Click the **Download/Install**button to install the selected plugins.
4. Restart your modeling tool. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Install custom pluginsInstalling custom plugins

If you are using custom-built plugin *.rdzip* files located on a local file system, network share, or a web server, you can add the plugins from the file by specifying its location via the **Resource/Plugin Manager**.

###### Adding plugins from a local file system or network shareAdding plugins from an *.rdzip* file located on a local file system or network share

To add plugins from an*.rdzip* file located on a local file system or network share

1. In the main menu, select Help > Resource/Plugin Manager .
2. In the Resource Location section, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dot_button.png'] button.
3. In the **Manage Resource Locations** dialog, click **Add**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] You can manage the list displayed in the **Manage Resource Locations** dialog by adding, removing, or reordering the resource locations.
4. The Select Resource Location or Distribution File dialog opens. Specify the location of the plugin bundle ( .rdzip ) file and click Open . The resource location is added.
5. Click **OK**to import the resources.
6. Select the check boxes for the plugins you want to install.
7. Click the **Download/Install**button to install the selected plugins.
8. Restart your modeling tool. You can extract an *.rdzip* file and then . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adding plugins from a web serverAdding plugins from a web server

You can also obtain the needed plugins from a web server if you do not have the .*rdzip* file but have been provided a URL by your license manager.

Installing plugins from a web serverTo add plugins from a web server

1. In the main menu, select Help > Resource/Plugin Manager .
2. In the Resource Location section, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dot_button.png'] button.
3. In the Manage Resource Locations dialog, click Add URL . The Resource Server URL dialog opens.
4. In the Resource Server URL dialog, type or paste the server address. [ATTACHMENT filename='adding_resource_server_url.png']
5. Click OK . The plugins from the selected location are listed in the Resource/Plugin Manager .

##### Other plugin installation optionsOther plugin installation options

The default and the recommended option is to use the pre-bundled *core.rdzip* file which is added automatically, however, you can also install individual plugins from their *.zip* files.

###### Installing an individual plugin by importing a *.zip*file

Installing an individual plugin by importing a .zip fileTo install an individual plugin by importing a *.zip*file

1. In the main menu, select Help > Resource/Plugin Manager .
2. Click the Import button and specify the location of the archived plugin file ( .zip ) and click Open . The plugin is extracted and installed automatically.
3. Restart your modeling tool. The **Import** button is used to install only an individual plugin by importing an archived plugin file (*.zip*). To install plugins from an *.rdzip* plugin bundle file, you need to add the *.rdzip* file by specifying its **Resource Location**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Installing an individual plugin by extracting a *.zip* file

Installing an individual plugin by extracting a .zip fileTo install an individual plugin by extracting a .*zip* file

1. Extract an archived plugin file ( .zip ) file to the same directory where your modeling tool is installed.
2. Start the modeling tool. The plugin is installed on your modeling tool.

#### TIP: Getting help

Getting help

If you run into any installation-related problems, try the following:

- Check the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Frequently Asked Questions' space='FAQ'] for known problems.
- Check the [No Magic Community forum.](https://community.nomagic.com/)
- Contact [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support' space='SUP'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54f93846-6f66-4af2-b246-194816a665da"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information about plugin installation in versions prior to the 2022x version, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing plugins in earlier versions" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b858e18f-011a-4709-a9f1-69cf40babe52"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Choose one of the following ways to install a plugin in a modeling tool:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link ac:anchor="Add pre-bundled plugin bundle"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Installing our commercial and no-cost plugins]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link ac:anchor="Install custom plugins"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Installing custom plugins]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding plugins from a local file system or network share"><ac:link-body>Adding plugins from an <em>.rdzip</em> file located on a local file system or network share</ac:link-body></ac:link></p></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding plugins from a web server" /></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link ac:anchor="Other plugin installation options" /></p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link ac:anchor="Installing an individual plugin by importing a .zip file"><ac:link-body>Installing an individual plugin by importing a <em>.zip</em> file</ac:link-body></ac:link></p></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Installing an individual plugin by extracting a .zip file"><ac:link-body>Installing an individual plugin by extracting a <em>.zip</em> file<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul><h3><span>Installing our commercial and no-cost plugins</span></h3><p>All available plugins provided by us, both commercial and no-cost, are accessible via the <em>core.rdzip</em> file, which is pre-bundled together with the installation files and is readily available in the <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong>. The available plugins depend on the modeling tool that you are using. The file is located at <em>&lt;tool_installation_directory&gt;\data\resourcemanager\distribution\core.rdzip</em>, however, it is added automatically to the <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong>.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="befc40f1-9f40-4872-a6ea-254e203acb2b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Add pre-bundled plugin bundle</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To install our commercial and no-cost plugins</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Help</strong> &gt; <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong>. The pre-bundled <em>core.rdzip</em> file containing all available plugins is loaded automatically.</li><li>Select the check boxes for the plugins you want to install.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target _mce_tagged_br">Click the <strong>Download/Install </strong>button to install the selected plugins.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Restart your modeling tool.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Install_a_plugin_through_the_pre-bundled_plugin_file.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing plugins" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2382a198-ede7-45b0-9d2f-ee3ae9cb9f79"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Install custom plugins</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Installing custom plugins</h3><p>If you are using custom-built plugin <em>.rdzip</em> files located on a local file system, network share, or a web server, you can add the plugins from the file by specifying its location via the <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong>.</p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ed2e0fa-0b78-464c-b125-03703c19c347"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Adding plugins from a local file system or network share</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Adding plugins from an <em>.rdzip</em> file located on a local file system or network share</h4><p>To add plugins from an<em> .rdzip</em> file located on a local file system or network share</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Help</strong> &gt; <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Resource Location</strong> section, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing plugins" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Manage Resource /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e487dbde-2d43-4af6-87b0-d386bdceeb13"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can manage the list displayed in the <strong>Manage Resource Locations</strong> dialog by adding, removing, or reordering the resource locations.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>The <strong>Select Resource Location or Distribution File</strong> dialog opens. Specify the location of the plugin bundle (<em>.rdzip</em>) file and click<strong> Open</strong>. The resource location is added.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target _mce_tagged_br">Click <strong>OK </strong>to import the resources.</p></li><li>Select the check boxes for the plugins you want to install. </li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target _mce_tagged_br">Click the <strong>Download/Install </strong>button to install the selected plugins.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Restart your modeling tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e58112d-1502-43f2-ad1b-0de0c7a2e82a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can extract an <em>.rdzip</em> file and then <ac:link ac:anchor="Installing an individual plugin by importing a .zip file"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[add selected plugins individually]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Installing_a_plugin_by_adding_a_plugin_bundle.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing plugins" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a446d22-3ea3-46a8-ad8e-ee20ab6ee5e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Adding plugins from a web server</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Adding plugins from a web server</h4><p>You can also obtain the needed plugins from a web server if you do not have the .<em>rdzip</em> file but have been provided a URL by your license manager.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="587cacf4-c529-411d-bbff-27e867399129"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Installing plugins from a web server The plugins from the selected location are listed in the <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong>.</li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7544c9c5-c555-4923-8cb4-56b406f84321"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other plugin installation options</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other plugin installation options</h3><p>The default and the recommended option is to <ac:link ac:anchor="Add pre-bundled plugin bundle"><ac:link-body>use the pre-bundled <em>core.rdzip</em> file</ac:link-body></ac:link> which is added automatically, however, you can also install individual plugins from their <em>.zip</em> files.</p><h4>Installing an individual plugin by importing a <em>.zip </em>file </h4><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4fb9169c-08e1-4b4a-a62a-84d4b1e068da"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Installing an individual plugin by importing a .zip file </ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To install an individual plugin by importing a <em>.zip </em>file </p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Help</strong> &gt; <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Import</strong> button and specify the location of the archived plugin file (<em>.zip</em>) and click <strong>Open</strong>. The plugin is extracted and installed automatically.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Restart your modeling tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f179563d-593b-43e4-a9f7-1ed3980b26ac"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Import</strong> button is used to install only an individual plugin by importing an archived plugin file (<em>.zip</em>). To install plugins from an <em>.rdzip</em> plugin bundle file, you need to add the <em>.rdzip</em> file by <ac:link ac:anchor="Adding plugins from a local file system or network share"><ac:link-body>specifying its <strong>Resource Location</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Installing_an_individual_plugin.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing plugins" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h4>Installing an individual plugin by extracting a <em>.zip</em> file</h4><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="723543ef-93f9-4cba-82d4-3330fe44ab16"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Installing an individual plugin by extracting a .zip file</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To install an individual plugin by extracting a .<em>zip</em> file</p><hr /><ol><li>Extract an archived plugin file (<em>.zip</em>) file to the same directory where your modeling tool is installed.</li><li>Start the modeling tool. The plugin is installed on your modeling tool.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="38679957-e001-4681-bd27-eb87f42edcce"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Getting help</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If you run into any installation-related problems, try the following:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Check the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="Frequently Asked Questions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[FAQ section]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">for known problems.</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Check the</span> <a class="external-link" href="https://community.nomagic.com/" rel="nofollow">No Magic Community forum<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span></a></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contact <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SUP" ri:content-title="Support" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customer support]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740551 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00138: (2026x) Installing plugins in earlier versions

- page_id: `272740551`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740551/2026x+Installing+plugins+in+earlier+versions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:19.937+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins installation > (2026x) Installing modeling tools and plugins > (2026x) Installing plugins
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For information about plugin installation in 2022x version and later, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installing plugins' space=''] page.

#### EXCERPT: Getting help

INLINE

Choose one of the following ways to install a plugin in a modeling tool:

- Install a plugin bundle (.rdzip) via the **Resource/Plugin Manager** dialog. For information on how to download the plugin bundle, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading installation files' space=''].
- Install an individually purchased archived plugin file (.zip) via the **Resource/Plugin Manager** dialog.
- .
- .

To install a plugin bundle (.rdzip) via the Resource/Plugin Manager dialog To install a plugin bundle (.rdzip) via the **Resource/Plugin Manager** dialog

1. Start your modeling tool.
2. From the modeling tool main menu, select Help > Resource/Plugin Manager .
3. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dot_button.png'] button.
4. In the Manage Resource Locations dialog, click Add . The Select Resource Location or Distribution File dialog opens.
5. Specify the location of a plugin bundle (.rdzip) file and click Open . The resource location is added.
6. Click **OK**to import resources.

You can extract the .rdzip file and then .

adding individual pluginsTo install an individual plugin from an archive file (.zip) via the **Resource/Plugin Manager** dialog

1. Start your modeling tool.
2. From the main menu of a modeling tool, select Help > Resource/Plugin Manager .
3. Click the Import button and specify the location of an archived plugin file (.zip) and click Open . The plugin is extracted and installed automatically.
4. Restart your modeling tool.

Install a plugin manually To install a plugin manually

1. Extract an archived plugin file (.zip) file to the same directory where your modeling tool is installed.
2. Start the modeling tool. The plugin is applied to your modeling tool.

You can also obtain the needed resources from a web server if you do not have the .*rdzip* file but have been provided with an URL by your license manager.

Obtain and install a plugin from a web server To specify the server where the resources and their descriptor file are located

1. Open Resource/Plugin Manager and click [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dot_button.png'] .
2. In the Manage Resource Locations dialog, click Add URL . The Resource Server URL dialog opens.
3. In the open Resource Server URL dialog, type or paste the server address. [ATTACHMENT filename='adding_resource_server_url.png']
4. Click OK .

The resources from the selected location are listed in the **Resource/Plugin Manager**.

#### TIP: Getting help

Getting help

If you run into any installation-related problems, try the following:

- Check the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Frequently Asked Questions' space='FAQ'] for known problems.
- Check the [No Magic Community forum](https://community.nomagic.com/).
- Contact [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support' space='SUP'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740519 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00140: (2026x) Java version support for 19.0 SP2

- page_id: `272740519`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740519/2026x+Java+version+support+for+19.0+SP2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:17.918+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Java version support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### INFO: Changing JVM version

Changing JVM version

For the information on how to change the JVM version, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) How to change JVM version' space='']

Starting with 19.0 SP2, all the modeling tools, plugins, and collaboration tools were tested with OpenJDK* as an alternative to Oracle JDK.

In the following table, please find the recommendations for the appropriate Java version.

| Product | Version | Recommended | Alternative | Included into installation files |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| MagicDrawCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise ArchitectureMagic Software ArchitectMagic Cyber Systems EngineerMagic Systems of Systems Architect | 19.0 SP2 | Oracle JDK 8Windows, Linux: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_202Mac: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_102 | OpenJDK 8(1.8.0_192) | Oracle JDK 8Windows: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_202Mac: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_102 |
| Teamwork CloudTeamwork Server | 19.0 SP2 | Oracle JDK 8Windows, Linux: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_202Mac: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_102 | OpenJDK 8(1.8.0_192) | Oracle JDK 8Windows: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_202Mac: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_102 |
| Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork CloudWeb Application PlatformResourcesTWCloud Admin Console NG | 19.0 SP2 | OpenJDK 11 (2018-09-25 update) | Oracle JDK 11 | OpenJDK 11 (2018-09-25 update) |
| Cameo Collaborator for Alfresco | 19.0 SP2 | Oracle JDK 8Windows, Linux: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_202Mac: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_102 | Oracle JDK 7 | Oracle JDK 7 |

***OpenJDK**. Based on the testing results, we recommend using OpenJDK by AdoptJdk.

See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) JVM recommendations for versions up to 19.0 SP1' space=''].

````html

For the information on how to change the JVM version, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) How to change JVM version' space='']

In the following table, please find the recommendations for the appropriate Java version for 19.0 SP3.

| Product | Version | Recommended | Alternative | Included into installation files |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| MagicDrawCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise ArchitectureMagic Software ArchitectMagic Cyber Systems EngineerMagic Systems of Systems Architect | 19.0 SP3 | Oracle JDK 8Windows, Linux: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_202Mac: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_102 | OpenJDK 8(1.8.0_222) | Oracle JDK 8Windows: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_202Mac: Oracle JDK 1.8.0_102 |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740504 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00143: (2026x) Java version support for 2021x

- page_id: `272740504`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740504/2026x+Java+version+support+for+2021x
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:16.603+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Java version support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### INFO: Changing JVM version

Changing JVM version

For information on how to change the JVM version, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) How to change JVM version' space='']

In the following table, please find the recommendations for the appropriate Java version for 2021x.

| Product | Version | Recommended and included in installation files** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| MagicDrawCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise ArchitectureMagic Software ArchitectMagic Cyber Systems EngineerMagic Systems of Systems Architect | 2021x | OpenJDK 11.0.9* (for all OS) |
| Teamwork Cloud | 2021x | OpenJDK 11.0.9* Cassandra 3.11.x requires JDK 8 (either Oracle or Open JDK). |
| Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork CloudWeb Application PlatformResourcesTWCloud Admin Console NG | 2021x | OpenJDK 14.0.1* |

***OpenJDK**. Based on the testing results, we recommend using OpenJDK HotSpot by AdoptJdk.

We strongly advise using the HotSpot distribution rather than OpenJ9. For more information, see "Help Me Choose" tips at [https://adoptopenjdk.net/](https://adoptopenjdk.net/).

**Java is included only in installation files for Windows and macOS. Linux installation files do not have built-in Java.

See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) JVM recommendations for versions up to 19.0 SP1' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740480 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00144: (2026x) Java version support for 2021x Refresh1

- page_id: `272740480`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740480/2026x+Java+version+support+for+2021x+Refresh1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:15.029+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Java version support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### INFO: Changing JVM version

Changing JVM version

For information on how to change the JVM version, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) How to change JVM version' space='']

In the following table, please find the recommendations for the appropriate Java version for 2021x Refresh1.

| Product | Version | Recommended and included in installation files** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| MagicDrawCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise ArchitectureMagic Software ArchitectMagic Cyber Systems EngineerMagic Systems of Systems Architect | 2021x Refresh1 | OpenJDK 11.0.10* (for all OS) |
| Teamwork Cloud | 2021x Refresh1 | OpenJDK 11.0.10* Cassandra 3.11.x requires JDK 8 (either Oracle or Open JDK). |
| Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork CloudWeb Application PlatformResourcesTWCloud Admin Console NG | 2021x Refresh1 | OpenJDK 15.0.2* |

***OpenJDK**. Based on the testing results, we recommend using OpenJDK HotSpot by AdoptJdk.

We strongly advise using the HotSpot distribution rather than OpenJ9. For more information, see "Help Me Choose" tips at [https://adoptopenjdk.net/](https://adoptopenjdk.net/).

**Java is included only in installation files for Windows and macOS. Linux installation files do not have built-in Java.

See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) JVM recommendations for versions up to 19.0 SP1' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### INFO: Changing JVM version

Changing JVM version

For information on how to change the JVM version, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) How to change JVM version' space='']

In the following table, please find the recommendations for the appropriate Java version for 2022x Refresh1.

| Product | Version | Recommended and included in installation files** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| MagicDrawCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise ArchitectureMagic Software ArchitectMagic Cyber Systems EngineerMagic Systems of Systems Architect | 2022x Refresh1 | OpenJDK 11.0.16+8* (for all OS***) |
| Teamwork Cloud | 2022x Refresh1 | OpenJDK 11.0.14.1+1* Cassandra 4.0.x requires JDK 11 (either Oracle or Open JDK). |
| Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork CloudWeb Application PlatformResourcesTWCloud Admin Console NG | 2022x Refresh1 | OpenJDK 17.0.4* |

***OpenJDK**. Based on the testing results, we recommend using Eclipse Temurin™ by ADOPTIUM.

Based on the testing results, we recommend using Eclipse Temurin™ by ADOPTIUM.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3cc266a-8856-4a28-b50e-e0eee0ce0f4b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>OpenJ9 should not be used; instead, the HotSpot distribution should be utilized. For more information, see &quot;Help Me Choose&quot; tips at <a href="https://adoptopenjdk.net/">https://adoptopenjdk.net/</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>**Java is included only in installation files for Windows and macOS. Linux installation files do not have built-in Java.</p><p>*** You should choose Java native to the macOS architecture (ARM or x86) in use.</p><hr /><p>See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) JVM recommendations for versions up to 19.0 SP1" /></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740449 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00149: (2026x) Java version support for 2024x

- page_id: `272740449`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740449/2026x+Java+version+support+for+2024x
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:13.678+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Java version support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### INFO: Changing JVM version

Changing JVM version

For information on how to change the JVM version, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) How to change JVM version' space='']

In the following table, please find the recommendations for the appropriate Java version for 2024x.

| Product | Version | Recommended and included in installation files** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| MagicDrawCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise ArchitectureMagic Software ArchitectMagic Cyber Systems EngineerMagic Systems of Systems Architect | 2024x | OpenJDK 17.0.8+7* (for all OS***) |
| Teamwork Cloud | 2024x | OpenJDK 17.0.8+7* Cassandra 4.1.x requires JDK 11 (either Oracle or Open JDK). |
| Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork CloudWeb Application PlatformResourcesTWCloud Admin Console NG | 2024x | OpenJDK 17.0.8 |

***OpenJDK**. Based on the testing results, we recommend using Eclipse Temurin™ by ADOPTIUM.

OpenJ9 should not be used; instead, the HotSpot distribution should be utilized. For more information, see "Help Me Choose" tips at [https://adoptopenjdk.net/](https://adoptopenjdk.net/).

**Java is included only in installation files for Windows and macOS. Linux installation files do not have built-in Java.

*** You should choose Java native to the macOS architecture (ARM or x86) in use.

See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) JVM recommendations for versions up to 19.0 SP1' space=''].

See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Zip exceptions thrown after Java version upgrade to 11.0.20+ or 17.0.8+' space='FAQ'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Based on the testing results, we recommend using Eclipse Temurin™ by ADOPTIUM.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3cc266a-8856-4a28-b50e-e0eee0ce0f4b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>OpenJ9 should not be used; instead, the HotSpot distribution should be utilized. For more information, see &quot;Help Me Choose&quot; tips at <a href="https://adoptopenjdk.net/">https://adoptopenjdk.net/</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>**Java is included only in installation files for Windows and macOS. Linux installation files do not have built-in Java.</p><p>*** You should choose Java native to the macOS architecture (ARM or x86) in use.</p><hr /><p>See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) JVM recommendations for versions up to 19.0 SP1" /></ac:link>.</p><p>See <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="Zip exceptions thrown after Java version upgrade to 11.0.20+ or 17.0.8+" /></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p>
