**Java is included only in installation files for Windows and macOS. Linux installation files do not have built-in Java.

*** You should choose Java native to the macOS architecture (ARM or x86) in use.

See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) JVM recommendations for versions up to 19.0 SP1' space=''].

See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Zip exceptions thrown after Java version upgrade to 11.0.20+ or 17.0.8+' space='FAQ'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e9a5568-7288-4242-8ce6-ebc91321df0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Changing JVM version</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information on how to change the JVM version, please see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) How to change JVM version" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In the following table, please find the recommendations for the appropriate Java version for 2026x.</p><table class="fixed-table wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 234.0px;" /><col style="width: 142.0px;" /><col style="width: 279.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Product</th><th>Version</th><th>Recommended and included in installation files**</th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><ul><li>MagicDraw</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture</li><li>Magic Software Architect</li><li>Magic Cyber Systems Engineer</li><li>Magic Systems of Systems Architect</li></ul></td><td>2026x</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>AdoptOpenJDK 21.0.8+9-LTS</strong> (Eclipse Temurin) with HotSpot JVM for all OSs.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>2026x HotFix 1</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>AdoptOpenJDK 21.0.9+10</strong></span></p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><ul><li>Teamwork Cloud</li><li>Magic Collaboration Studio</li></ul></td><td>2026x</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>AdoptOpenJDK 21.0.8+9-LTS</strong> (Eclipse Temurin) with HotSpot JVM.</span></p><p>Cassandra 4.1.x requires <strong>JDK 11</strong> (either Oracle or Open JDK).</p></td></tr><tr><td>2026x HotFix 1</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>AdoptOpenJDK 21.0.9+10</strong></span></p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><ul><li>Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud</li><li>Web Application Platform</li><li>Resources</li><li>TWCloud Admin Console NG</li></ul></td><td>2026x</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>AdoptOpenJDK 21.0.8+9-LTS</strong> (Eclipse Temurin) with HotSpot JVM.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>2026x HotFix 1</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>AdoptOpenJDK 21.0.9+10</strong></span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p>*<strong>OpenJDK</strong>. Based on the testing results, we recommend using Eclipse Temurin™ by ADOPTIUM.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3cc266a-8856-4a28-b50e-e0eee0ce0f4b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>OpenJ9 should not be used; instead, the HotSpot distribution should be utilized. For more information, see &quot;Help Me Choose&quot; tips at <a href="https://adoptopenjdk.net/">https://adoptopenjdk.net/</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>**Java is included only in installation files for Windows and macOS. Linux installation files do not have built-in Java.</p><p>*** You should choose Java native to the macOS architecture (ARM or x86) in use.</p><hr /><p>See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) JVM recommendations for versions up to 19.0 SP1" /></ac:link>.</p><p>See <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="Zip exceptions thrown after Java version upgrade to 11.0.20+ or 17.0.8+" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

## PAGE 00154: (2026x) JVM recommendations for versions up to 19.0 SP1

- page_id: `272740523`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740523/2026x+JVM+recommendations+for+versions+up+to+19.0+SP1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:18.127+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Java version support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**MagicDraw** and **Cameo Suite** products are Java based. 
The following table lists the **recommended* Java versions.**

* Most of testing was performed with these versions

****

**Please note, Oracle Java SE9 is not supported*.***

| Product version | Recommended Java version | Not supported Java versions |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740436 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00155: (2026x) License Manager

- page_id: `272740436`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740436/2026x+License+Manager
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:13.263+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) Basic concepts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two ways to view the **License Manager** dialog:

- Starting the modeling tool for the very first time.
- Opening the dialog from the modeling tool's Help menu.

When you start the modeling tool for the first time, you will see the following dialog:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The License Manager dialog

In this dialog, you can select one of the license addition options according to the license you have.

| Button name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use Floating License | If you have the credentials for a Floating license server, click to . |
| Select License Key File | If you have a seat, education, student, or non-profit research license, click to . |
| Use in Reader Mode | To open the modeling tool in Reader Mode to read projects, click to . |
| Show Target ID | of your machine to be able to tie it with the license. |

When you need to change the license key file or obtain a floating license, you can open the**License Manager** dialog from the modeling tool menu (**Help** > **License Manager**).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740559 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00157: (2026x) Managed licensing

- page_id: `272740559`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740559/2026x+Managed+licensing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:20.424+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) Basic concepts > (2026x) License types
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

DSLS (Dassault Systèmes License Server) is a licensing system developed by Dassault Systèmes that, in addition to seat and floating, also provides a managed licensing option.

Managed DSLS is also known as Managed Licensing Service.

With Managed DSLS, an on-premise customer no longer needs a physical machine to install DS License Server (DSLS). The license server is managed by Dassault Systèmes and located in the Dassault Systèmes Cloud datacenter.

Client machines authorized by the customer (authentication file) and located on the customer’s premises access the DSLS remotely. An administrator on the customer’s premises access the DSLS through a web interface using a login and a password.

For more information, please consult:

- *“Using The Managed Licensing Service” in the* DSLS Installation and Configuration Guide
- Managed DSLS Eligibility
- Managed DSLS maintenance

**Please contact your Dassault Systèmes sales representative in case you have an interest in Managed DSLS service.**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The license server is managed by Dassault Systèmes and located in the Dassault Systèmes Cloud datacenter.</p><p class="bodytext" style="text-align: left;">Client machines authorized by the customer (authentication file) and located on the customer’s premises access the DSLS remotely. An administrator on the customer’s premises access the DSLS through a web interface using a login and a password.</p><p class="bodytext" style="text-align: left;">For more information, please consult:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><strong><em>“Using The Managed Licensing Service” in the<span> </span></em></strong><a href="http://media.3ds.com/support/documentation/product/DS_License_Server/R2024x/DSLS-installation-and-configuration-guide.pdf?_gl=1*168vkyh*_ga*MjA1MDE4MDI3LjE2NjkwMTM5OTY.*_ga_DYJDKXYEZ4*MTcwMDEyMzUxMS40OS4wLjE3MDAxMjM1MTEuNjAuMC4w">DSLS Installation and Configuration Guide</a></li><li><a title="Opens internal link in current window" href="https://www.3ds.com/support/3dexperience-platform-on-cloud-support/eligibility/managed-dsls-eligibility/" style="text-decoration: none;">Managed DSLS Eligibility</a></li><li><a title="Opens external link in new window" style="text-decoration: none;" href="https://www.3ds.com/support/news/?woc=%7B%22support%20category%22%3A%5B%22support%20category%2Fmanaged%20dsls%20maintenance%22%5D%7D">Managed DSLS maintenance</a></li></ul><p class="bodytext" style="text-align: left;"><strong>Please contact your Dassault Systèmes sales representative in case you have an interest in Managed DSLS service.</strong></p>

## PAGE 00159: (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing

- page_id: `272740420`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740420/2026x+Modeling+tools+and+plugins+licensing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:12.912+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

50%

The product license is identified by the license key. The license key corresponds to:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Basic concepts' space='']: DSLS, FlexNet.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Basic concepts' space='']: Nodelock/Seat, Floating, Managed.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Basic concepts' space='']: Commercial, Academic, Student, Non-Profit Research.

Availability:

- FlexNet:
  - Seat
  - Floating
- DSLS:
  - Nodelock
  - Floating
  - Managed

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Licensing scheme

##### Licensing workflow

###### FlexNet Seat / DSLS Nodelock license

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Determine the Target ID' space=''] .
2. Provide it to your sales representative.
3. Get a license key.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding the license key' space='']

###### Floating license

**FlexNet server license**

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed' space=''] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed' space='']
2. Provide it to your sales representative.
3. Get a floating license key and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding a license to the FlexNet server' space='']. Typically, this step is performed by the license server administrator.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool' space='']

**DSLS server license**

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Obtaining license keys' space=''] .
2. Provide it to your sales representative.
3. Get a floating license key and add it to the floating license server.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool' space='']

###### Managed license

Contact your Dassault Systèmes sales representative if you have an interest in Managed DSLS service. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managed licensing' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e7a93fd-0a3b-4af7-8947-6dfdff73b52b"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="773003c0-77f0-45a9-a434-a80be47c9f05"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The product license is identified by the license key. The license key corresponds to:</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Basic concepts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>: DSLS, FlexNet.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Basic concepts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License type]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>: Nodelock/Seat, Floating, Managed.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Basic concepts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License category]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>: Commercial, Academic, Student, Non-Profit Research.</p><p><br /></p><p>Availability:</p><ul><li data-uuid="9d286032-3e38-443e-aaf0-87907e8b3337">FlexNet: <ul><li>Seat</li><li>Floating</li></ul></li><li <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740408 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00160: (2026x) Modeling tools bundle

- page_id: `272740408`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740408/2026x+Modeling+tools+bundle
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:12.561+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins installation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a modeling tools bundle (Windows OS)

Here, you can find the instructions on how to create a modeling tools bundle together with already installed plugins and defined options. A modeling tools bundle can be created by the Administrator or any responsible person and then provided to the company’s employees.

###### Advantages of using the modeling tools bundle

- Employees will receive the modeling tools bundle with already installed plugins - they will not need to spend time searching and installing required plugins.
- Employees will have the modeling tools bundle with already predefined [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MT'] .

###### The most important environment options usually are

- Security certificates to enable secure communication between a modeling tool and Teamwork Cloud. See the bottom of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling secure connection between client and server' space='MCS'] page.
- Floating License Server information
- Teamwork Cloud Server information
- Cameo Collaborator Server information

###### The modeling tools bundle can be prepared in the following way

1. The administrator installs a modeling tool on their computer.
2. The administrator installs all the required plugins and defines the options.
3. The administrator compresses the modeling tool installation folder.
4. The administrator extracts the modeling tools bundle on employee machines.

The employees need to start the modeling tool, and if they are using a seat license, they will need to apply and activate the license (or apply the already activated license provided for their machine).

For detailed steps, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating modeling tools bundle' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using modeling tools bundle' space=''] pages.

###### Installation directories

By default, when you install a modeling tool, it writes to two separate directories:

1. Installation directory – C:\Program Files\<modeling tool name> (default).
2. Configuration directory – C:\Users\<your username>\AppData\Local\.<modeling_tool>\<version number>

###### Configuration files

The configuration files' locations are in folders named corresponding to the modeling tool name, e.g., */.cameo.systems.modeler/*for Cameo Systems Modeler and */cameoea/*for Cameo Enterprise Architecture. 
The properties files' names in the *bin* directory are also named *csm.properties* and *cameoea.properties,* corresponding to the product names.

###### Licensing

- Floating License . This works best for a pre-built bundle as the bundle can be configured with the license server information as part of the bundle. If your company uses floating licenses, the individuals will not have to use an individual seat license.
- Seat License .

For more information regarding available licensing options, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740595 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00161: (2026x) Moving nodelocked modeling tool to another machine

- page_id: `272740595`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740595/2026x+Moving+nodelocked+modeling+tool+to+another+machine
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:22.093+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

To move the nodelocked (locked to the particular machine) license from one machine to another:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installing modeling tools' space=''] the modeling tool on a new machine.
2. Request a license key for a new machine (determine the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Determine the Target ID' space=''] and provide it to your sales representative).
3. Get a new license key.
4. Start the modeling tool and add a license key.

Please note that you do not need to request for a new license key if you use a floating/shareable license.

- page_id: `272740512`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

**Prerequisites**

- You have a license key dedicated for a new machine.

movingTo move the FlexNet license server to another machine

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Uninstalling FlexNet license server' space=''] from the current machine.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0' space=''] server on a new machine.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding a license to the FlexNet server' space=''] to the FlexNet server.

To upgrade the FlexNet license server to a new version

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Uninstalling FlexNet license server' space=''] from the current machine.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installing FlexNet 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0' space=''] server version on the machine.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding a license to the FlexNet server' space=''] to the FlexNet server.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740584 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00163: (2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool

- page_id: `272740584`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740584/2026x+Obtaining+a+floating+license+for+a+modeling+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:21.683+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

##### Prerequisites

- The floating license server ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DSLS installation and licensing' space=''] ) is installed and started.
- You have the floating license server name and port (provided by your system or license server administrator).

##### Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool

To obtain a floating license for a modeling tool

1. Start the modeling tool.
2. When the **License Manager** dialog opens, click the **Use Floating License** button. To open this dialog manually, do one of the following:In the main menu, click**Help**> **License Manager**. The**License Manager**dialog opens.In the main menu, click **Help**> **Resource/Plugin Manager**. In the**Resource/Plugin Manager**dialog, click the **Manage Licenses**button. The**License Manager**dialog opens.
3. In the **License Server Connection** dialog, select one of the following:
  1. **FlexNet**. Enter the floating license server name and port (provided by your system or license server administrator). Click **OK**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you want to use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) FlexNet Three-Server Redundancy - Triad License Server' space='']**Three-Server Redundancy** (Triad License Server), follow this format: 
**Server Name:** hostname1,hostname2,hostname3 (where *hostname1*is the master of the triad)**Port:** 1101 (default port embedded in the floating license key file)If you want to use different Cameo vendor daemon ports for different triad servers, use this format: 
**Server Name:** hostname1,port2@hostname2,port3@hostname3 
**Port:** port1
  2. **DSLS**. Select one of the options:
    1. **Organization defined**. Provides access via the licensing servers specified in a file on the client's computer licensing directory. Can be either set by the user or by the server administrators up front. Click **OK**.
    2. **Custom**. Input the server address (must include the port number, which is 4085 by default, e.g., myaddress:4085). Click **OK**.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If the**Automatically login each time <modeling tool> is started** check box is selected, the **License Server Connection** dialog will not be displayed, and the licenses will be obtained automatically. To reset the licenses or select additional ones, do the following:In the main menu, click **Options** > **Environment**. The**Environment Options** dialog opens.In the **Floating** options group, set the **Auto Login to the License Server** option to *false*.Restart the modeling tool.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the **Select Edition/Plugin** dialog, select the modeling tool edition, the plugins you want to use, and the floating license connection type: online or offline ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Basic concepts' space='']). Click **OK**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Offline license supportDSLS offline (borrowed) licenses are not supported on macOS/Linux. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DSLS installation and licensing' space=''] page.FlexNet license borrowing is not supported on macOS machines running with ARM Java. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing' space=''] page. To obtain a floating license for your plugin, you must install it in your modeling tool first. Once the plugin is installed, you will find it listed under **Plugins** in the **Select Edition/Plugin** window. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Installing modeling tools and plugins' space=''] To check the license information, select **Help** > **About** and click the **Licensing** tab.

##### Finishing the offline floating license session

To finish an offline floating license session earlier

1. In the main menu, click Help > Finish Offline License Session . [ATTACHMENT filename='finish offline license session.png']
2. Click Yes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

In the<span> </span><strong>Resource/Plugin Manager<span> </span></strong>dialog, click the <strong>Manage Licenses</strong><span> </span>button. The<span> </span><strong>License Manager<span> </span></strong>dialog opens.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="9dbda8e8-9d09-45f5-805a-e772b955a089"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>License Server Connection</strong> dialog, select one of the following:</p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>FlexNet</strong>. E<span>nter the floating license server name and port (provided by your system or license server administrator). Click </span> <strong>OK</strong><span>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="license_server_connection_dialog_flexnet.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a613b0cd-b9cb-4436-93ac-15eb548d11d2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FlexNet Three-Server Redundancy - Triad License Server" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Three-Server Redundancy</strong> (Triad License Server)</ac:link-body></ac:link>, follow this format:<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Server Name:</strong> hostname1,hostname2,hostname3 (where <em>hostname1 </em>is the master of the triad)<br /></span><strong>Port:</strong> 1101 (default port embedded in the floating license key file)</p><p>If you want to use different Cameo vendor daemon ports for different triad servers, use this format:<br /><strong>Server Name:</strong> hostname1,port2@hostname2,port3@hostname3<br /><strong>Port:</strong> port1</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>DSLS</strong>. Select one of the options:</p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Organization defined</strong>. Provides access via the licensing servers specified in a file on the client's computer licensing directory. Can be either set by the user or by the server administrators up front. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Custom</strong>. Input the server address (must include the port number, which is 4085 by default, e.g., myaddress:4085). Click <strong>OK</strong>.<span> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="license_server_connection_dialog_dsls.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ae8e327-c03e-4d01-b627-376ea2507685"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the<strong> Automatically login each time &lt;modeling tool&gt; is started</strong> check box is selected, the <strong>License Server Connection</strong> dialog will not be displayed, and the licenses will be obtained automatically. To reset the licenses or select additional ones, do the following:</p><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. The<strong> Environment Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Floating</strong> options group, set the <strong>Auto Login to the License Server</strong> option to <em>false</em>.</li><li>Restart the modeling tool.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="license_server_connection_dialog_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="ff3ed711-0d70-42be-9362-8662013cebd4"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Edition/Plugin</strong> dialog, select the modeling tool edition, the plugins you want to use, and the floating license connection type: online or offline (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Basic concepts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[borrowing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_edition_plugin.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="041a9295-b12c-4472-b65b-4bb7dd1d1a25"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Offline license support</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>DSLS offline (borrowed) licenses are not supported on macOS/Linux. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DSLS installation and licensing" /></ac:link> page.</span></li><li><p>FlexNet license borrowing is not supported on macOS machines running with ARM Java. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing" /></ac:link> page.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a923a913-2942-488b-aca1-b4cb550b627b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To obtain a floating license for your plugin, you must install it in your modeling tool first. Once the plugin is installed, you will find it listed under <strong>Plugins</strong> in the <strong>Select Edition/Plugin</strong> window. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Installing modeling tools and plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Click here to learn how to install a plugin. ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90b5580c-663d-45a2-bb4e-1c2e2f7b0d6e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To check the license information, select <strong>Help</strong> &gt; <strong>About</strong> and click the <strong>Licensing</strong> tab.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Finishing the offline floating license session</h3><p>To finish an offline floating license session earlier</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8d937b1c-00a2-4647-858f-1a1cd0e26621"><span>In the main menu, click</span> <strong> Help</strong> <span> &gt;</span> <strong> Finish Offline License Session</strong><span>.</span> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="finish offline license session.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Obtaining a floating license for a modeling tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="d53bb68d-b76c-450a-aa50-10f71c15facc">Click <strong>Yes.</strong></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740572 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00164: (2026x) Obtaining license keys

- page_id: `272740572`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740572/2026x+Obtaining+license+keys
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:21.046+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

Software license keys can be obtained by contacting your Dassault Systèmes Sales Contact or Distributor with the necessary technical information. Please follow the procedures described below.

##### Requesting a FlexNet license key

To request a FlexNet license key

1. Determine the Host ID :
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Determine the Target ID' space=''] .
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed' space=''] .
2. Send this information to your Dassault Systèmes Sales Contact or Distributor
  1. by filling and sending the license key request form (XLS) .
  2. or by sending the Host ID directly.

##### Requesting a DSLS license key

To request a DSLS license key

1. Determine the Computer ID for the machine of a node-lock based license or the concurrent/shareable (floating) license:
  1. Download and extract the contents of the following ZIP file to the computer on which you will install the DSLS license key: DSLS target ID executable
  2. Run this command to identify the Computer ID: *DSLicTarget.exe –t* For an alternative option to determine the **Computer ID**for the machine of a node-lock based license, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Determine the Target ID' space=''] page.
2. Send this information to your Dassault Systèmes Sales Contact or Distributor
  1. by filling and sending the license key request form (XLS) .
  2. or by sending the Computer ID directly.

For more information, see:

- Dassault Systèmes License Server and License Keys
- Software License Keys

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

license or the concurrent/shareable (floating) license:<ol><li>Download and extract the contents of the following ZIP file to the computer on which you will install the DSLS license key: <a <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740447 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00165: (2026x) Product trigrams

- page_id: `272740447`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740447/2026x+Product+trigrams

- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:13.604+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) DSLS installation and licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

With DSLS, product licenses are checked out using trigrams instead of the full product name. See the tables below to knowthe specific trigrams denoting each product.

##### CATIA Magic portfolio

| Application | Trigram |  | Plugin | Trigram |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Magic Systems of Systems Architect | MYH |  | Magic Systems Cybersecurity Designer | MAK |
| Magic Systems of Systems Architect for Non-Profit Research | MYH-NPR |  | Magic Systems Cybersecurity Designer for Education | MAK-EDU |
| Magic Systems of Systems Architect for Education | MYH-EDU |  | Magic Systems Cybersecurity Designer for Non-Profit Research | MAK-NPR |
| Magic Systems of Systems Architect Student Edition | MYH-STU |  | Magic Model Analyst | MSI |
| Magic Cyber Systems Engineer | MAH |  | Magic Model Analyst Student Edition | MSI-STU |
| Magic Cyber Systems Engineer for Non-Profit Research | MAH-NPR |  | Magic Model Analyst for Education | MSI-EDU |
| Magic Software Architect | MWA |  | Magic Model Analyst for Non-Profit Research | MSI-NPR |
| Magic Collaboration Studio | MCS |  | Magic Alf Analyst | MAF |
| Magic Collaboration Studio Entry | MC5 |  | Magic Real-Time Communication Designer | MRC |
| Magic Collaboration Studio for Education | MCS-EDU |  | Magic Concept Modeler | MCO |
| Magic Collaboration 5 Connections pack | MCY |  | Magic Systems EE Architect for Education | MAJ-EDU |
|  |  | Magic Systems EE Architect for Non-Profit Research | MAJ-NPR |  |
|  | Magic Systems Safety Engineer | MAL |  |  |
|  | Magic Systems Safety Engineer for Education | MAL-EDU |  |  |
|  | Magic Systems Safety Engineer Non-Profit Research | MAL-NPR |  |  |

| Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, 5 Connections | M2T-05 |  | Cameo Simulation Toolkit Plugin | M7S |

| Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, 25 Connections | M2T-25 |  | Merge Plugin | M7G |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740535 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00166: (2026x) Reader Mode activation

- page_id: `272740535`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740535/2026x+Reader+Mode+activation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:18.758+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins installation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The modeling tool Reader Mode is used to read projects. You will be able to open projects, including server projects, view diagrams, print, and copy as images. The reader mode is available for all the modeling tools.

##### Prerequisites

- You have the modeling tool installed.

For information about how to download the installation files, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading Reader installation files' space=''].

##### Start using the Reader Mode for the first time

To start using the Reader Mode for the first time

1. Start the modeling tool for the first time. The License Manager dialog is displayed.
2. In the License Manager dialog, click the Use in Reader Mode button. The License Agreement window opens.
3. In the License Agreement window, click the I Agree button.

If you were working with the modeling tool's licensed version but need to switch to the Reader Mode, follow the steps of the procedure below.

##### Switching to the Reader Mode

To switch to the Reader Mode

1. In the modeling tool's main menu, click Help > License Manager .
2. In the open License Manager dialog, click the Use in Reader Mode button. The License Agreement window opens.
3. In the License Agreement window, click the I Agree button.
4. Restart the modeling tool.

You can always go back from using the Reader Mode to the licensed modeling tool version.

##### Switching back to the licensed version

To switch back to the licensed version

1. In the modeling tool's main menu, click Help > License Manager .
2. In the open License Manager dialog, do one of the following:
  1. Click the Use Floating License button. Restart the modeling tool.
  2. Click the Select License Key File button. Add the license key and restart the modeling tool.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: left;">The modeling tool Reader Mode is used to read projects. You will be able to open projects, including server projects, view diagrams, print, and copy as images. The reader mode is available for all the modeling tools.</p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Prerequisites</h3><ul><li style="text-align: left;">You have the modeling tool installed.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a580dd0-15de-41ad-a2af-b55ae7c7dbb7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information about how to download the installation files, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Downloading Reader installation files" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 style="text-align: left;">Start using the Reader Mode for the first time</h3><p style="text-align: left;">To start using the Reader Mode for the first time</p><hr /><ol><li style="text-align: left;">Start the modeling tool for the first time. The <strong>License Manager</strong> dialog is displayed.</li><li style="text-align: left;">In the <strong>License Manager</strong> dialog, click the <strong>Use in Reader Mode</strong> button. The License Agreement window opens.</li><li style="text-align: left;">In the License Agreement window, click the <strong>I Agree</strong> button.</li></ol><p>If you were working with the modeling tool's licensed version but need to switch to the Reader Mode, follow the steps of the procedure below.</p><h3>Switching to the Reader Mode</h3><p>To switch to the Reader Mode</p><hr /><ol><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Help </strong>&gt; <strong>License Manager</strong>.</li><li>In the open <strong>License Manager</strong> dialog, click the <strong>Use in Reader Mode</strong> button. The License Agreement window opens.</li><li>In the License Agreement window, click the <strong>I Agree</strong> button.</li><li>Restart the modeling tool.</li></ol><p style="text-align: left;">You can always go back from using the Reader Mode to the licensed modeling tool version. </p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Switching back to the licensed version</h3><p>To switch back to the licensed version</p><hr /><ol><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Help </strong>&gt; <strong>License Manager</strong>.</li><li>In the open <strong>License Manager</strong> dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>Click the <strong>Use Floating License</strong> button. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740500 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00167: (2026x) Replacing a license key on the FlexNet license server

- page_id: `272740500`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740500/2026x+Replacing+a+license+key+on+the+FlexNet+license+server
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:16.091+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: To add a new license file to the floating license server by copying it directly to a specific location

INLINE

You must replace the old floating license key with a new one if:

- You upgrade products to the new version that uses the floating license.
- The Ethernet Address ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed' space=''] ) of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed changes.

To replace a license key

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Adding a license to the FlexNet server' space=''] (or use the alternative*).
2. Restart the Vendor Daemon - Cameo:
  1. Connect to the FlexNet License Server management Web interface. The default address is*[http://localhost:8090/](http://localhost:8090/)*or use your server name (*http://<server name>:8090*)
  2. Click the Vendor Daemon Configuration tab.
  3. In the Vendor Daemons list, click the desired daemon and start in again.
  4. Save changes.

* You can copy the new license key directly to a specific location in the floating license server installation folder rather than importing the file by using *lmadmin*. Use this method as an alternative if you cannot import the license key.

#### EXPAND: To add a new license file to the floating license server by copying it directly to a specific location

To add a new license file to the floating license server by copying it directly to a specific location

1. Go to the folder the new license file is stored in and copy the file.
2. Go to <floating license server installation directory>\licenses\cameo and paste the file.
3. Connect to the FlexNet License Server management Web interface. Use the default address ([http://localhost:8090](http://localhost:8090/)) or your server name (http://<server name>:8090).
4. Sign in as the administrator.
5. On the left side menu, click Vendor Daemon Configuration .
6. In the appropriate cameo vendor daemon row, click the Administer button.
7. Click the Reread License Files button. If the license file is successfully imported, you will see an appropriate message on the webpage.
8. Check the active licenses (see the following procedure).
9. If the active licenses are not updated, please restart the floating server.

To check active licenses

1. Connect to the FlexNet License Server management Web interface. The default address is http://localhost:8090/ or use your server name ( http://<server name>:8090 ).
2. Open the **Dashboard.**
3. Click Concurrent. All active licenses are listed in a table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740557 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00168: (2026x) Seat licensing

- page_id: `272740557`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740557/2026x+Seat+licensing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:20.310+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) Basic concepts > (2026x) License types
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Seat License, also known as a Standalone or Machine license, enables the use of modeling tools on a single, designated machine where the license is installed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p data-pm-slice="0 0 []">A Seat License, also known as a Standalone or Machine license, enables the use of modeling tools on a single, designated machine where the license is installed.</p>
````

| CPU | Equivalent or better than Intel Core i3-9100T or AMD counterpart (x86-64 architecture), Apple Intel x86-64,Apple M1 or higher. |  |

| Project Elements Count(including used projects) | Allocated Memory 2 |  |
| Earlier versions | 2021x and later 1 |  |
| 1.5 million | 1 million | 6GB |
| 2.5 million | 2 million | 7GB |
| 5 million | 3.5 million | 11GB |
| Disk space | The recommended disk space for installation and configuration files is 3GB or more, depending on the used plugins, project type (local or server), and project size. In addition to this, large server projects require extra disk space for configuration files: Project Elements Count(including used projects)Disk SpaceEarlier versions2021x and later1.5 million1 million6GB2.5 million2 million10GB5 million3.5 million30GBThe amount of required disk space proportionally increases if working on multiple projects.Note: Element History and Content History features usage require significantly more disk space due to intensive caching at the client-side.Depending on the feature usage scenario (e.g., retrieving content history from old/distant project versions multiple times), the disk space might increase by up to 100GB.Disk space requirements for working with SysML v2 projectsThe table below shows the required disk space for working with SysML v2 projects in the local repository.Element countRequired disk space in the local repository for the initial project versionEach subsequent version1M1GB200MB2M2GB400MB3M3GB600MB5M4GB1,2GB 13,5M (1,1M essential elements)10GB4GB |  |

| Element count | Required disk space in the local repository for the initial project version | Each subsequent version |
| 1M | 1GB | 200MB |
| 2M | 2GB | 400MB |
| 3M | 3GB | 600MB |
| 5M | 4GB | 1,2GB |
| 13,5M (1,1M essential elements) | 10GB | 4GB |
| Display resolution** | Full HD (1920x1080) or higher |  |
| Operating Systems | Any Java SE 21-compatible operating system (Windows 11, Windows Server, Linux (RedHat 8, RedHat 9, Oracle 9), Mac OS (Tahoe, Sequoia, Sonoma). |  |
| Java Virtual Machine (JVM) version |  |  |

* Magic Software Architect, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Magic Systems of Systems Architect, MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, Cameo Enterprise Architecture

** 800x600, 1280x1024 display resolutions are compatible with a laptop or a projector.

##### FLEXnet License Server

For system requirements, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading installation files' space=''] of the specific version.

##### DSLS License Server

For system requirements, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DSLS installation and licensing' space=''] page and the [DSLS Installation and Configuration Guide](http://media.3ds.com/support/documentation/product/DS_License_Server/2023x/DSLS_Installation_and_Configuration_Guide_23x.pdf?_gl=1*1b9rvxw*_ga*MjA1MDE4MDI3LjE2NjkwMTM5OTY.*_ga_DYJDKXYEZ4*MTY5MzIyODIzOS4zMi4wLjE2OTMyMjgyMzkuNjAuMC4w).

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before installing Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud, ensure that the system requirements described in this chapter are met.

##### Recommended system and storage requirements

#### NOTE: Multi-Node Clusters

Note on system requirements

System requirements are dictated by the intended deployment, taking into account the overall load which the environment will experience, including:

- Number of concurrent users
- Level of activity (commits) per day
- Overall number and size of the projects stored in Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud.

The database (Cassandra) can be located on the same server as Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud or on a separate server. Storage requirements apply only to the node where the database is located. The product hosting nodes can be virtualized without any issues if the host is not oversubscribed on its resources.

#### INFO: Multi-Node Clusters

Multi-Node Clusters

Recommended minimum sizing stated above applies to each node in a multi-node cluster.

#### NOTE: Note on storage requirements

Note on storage requirements

For on-premises deployment, Apache Cassandra is designed to run on multiple independent servers. Each Cassandra node should have Directly Attached Storage (DAS) composed of NVMe drives for optimum performance. Traditional network-attached storage solutions that rely on IP-based connections (iSCSI, NFS, or SAN) are not recommended for on-premises deployments, as they introduce additional latencies and anti-patterns. To learn more, see the following topics:

- Apache Cassandra Hardware Choices
- DataStax Apache Cassandra Capacity Planning Guide

For cloud deployment, use storage technologies that have been benchmarked for Cassandra. Modern solid-state/flash arrays with fast interconnects are preferred. Note that Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud is not compatible with managed Cassandra services, such as Amazon Keyspaces. To learn more, see the following topics:

- AWS EBS Cassandra Best Practices
- DataStax AWS EC2 Recommendations

| Resource requirements by deployment type |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Deployment type | Recommended system requirements | Recommended storage requirements |
| Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud AND Cassandra | 96 -128 GB ECC RAM>=16 processor threads (equivalent or better than the Intel Xeon E-2278G, or their AMD counterparts, supporting x86-64 architecture)1TB NVMe/SSD DAS storage | 3 Solid State Drives with high IOPS (recommended ~400K)NVMe or SSD drives are highly suitable due to their low latency and high throughput. 3 separate disk mountsData, commit logs, and applications have different access patterns and should reside on separate disks to avoid I/O contention. For a detailed example, please see |
| Only Cassandra | 48 - 64 GB ECC RAM>=8 processor threads (equivalent or better than the Intel Xeon E-2234, or their AMD counterparts, supporting x86-64 architecture)1TB NVMe/SSD DAS storage |  |
| Only Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud | 48 - 64 GB ECC RAM>=8 processor threads (equivalent or better than the Intel Xeon E-2234, or their AMD counterparts, supporting x86-64 architecture)>250GB storage |  |

##### Software requirements

Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud supports the following operating systems:

- Linux 64-bit RedHat 9, RedHat 8, Oracle Linux 8.
- Windows 2016 or Windows 2019.

The Linux operating system is highly recommended for the product deployment. Cassandra 4 and later versions do not have native Windows support.

For a fully working environment, you will also need the following:

- Cassandra is compatible with OpenJDK 17 (tested with Eclipse Temurin™ by ADOPTIUM 17.0.16)
- Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud is compatible with OpenJDK 21.0 (tested with Eclipse Temurin™ by ADOPTIUM 21.0.8)
- **IMPORTANT**. To use modeling tools with DSLS licenses,Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable must be installed (see[Latest supported Visual C++ Redistributable downloads](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/windows/latest-supported-vc-redist?view=msvc-170#visual-studio-2015-2017-2019-and-2022)). This applies to Windows OS only.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='FlexNet license server installation and licensing' space='ILTWRT'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DSLS installation and licensing' space='ILTWRT'] license server
- Cassandra 5.0 (tested with 5.0.5)
- A static IP address for each node.
- Open ports 1101, 2181, 2552, 7000, 7001, 7199, 9042, and 9142 between servers in a cluster.
- Open ports 3579, 8111, 8443, and 10002 (default) for clients. The port number 10002 can be changed according to the port assigned to secure connections between the client software and Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud.

The following table lists the ports that Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud services use and their descriptions. Port traffic is TCP unless otherwise specified.

| Service | Port | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| FlexNet server (lmadmin) | 1101 | FLEXnet server port |
|  | 8090 | License management web interface HTTP port |
|  | 27000-27009 | Internal license server manager port |
|  |  |  |
| Cassandra | 7000 | Internode cluster communication port (not used if TLS is enabled) |
|  | 7199 | JMX monitoring port of the Cassandra node |
|  | 9042 | Native client port for processing database operations |
|  |  |  |
| Magic Collaboration Studio / Teamwork Cloud | 2468 | JMX metrics reporting port for monitoring stack |
|  | 2552 | The product's default remote server port |
|  | 3579 | The default product port when SSL is not enabled |
|  | 8111 | REST API HTTPS port |
|  | 10002 | The default port for encrypted TLS client-server communication. |
|  |  |  |
| Web Application Platform | 8443 | Web Application Platform HTTPS port (Authentication, Collaborator…) |
|  |  |  |
| Zookeeper | 2181 | Zookeeper internal port |

#### INFO: If deploying on Amazon EC2

If deploying on Amazon EC2

When deploying on

, we recommend using the

, r5-2xlarge, or i3-2xlarge instances. Depending on the workload, you may want to go to the -4xlarge instances, but for most users, the -2xlarge will suffice. The m5 instances meet the minimum system requirements and will be acceptable for small deployments. The r5 instances provide more memory for the same CPU density. The i3 instances should be used when workloads have a higher level of user concurrency due to the significantly improved performance of the ephemeral NVMe storage.

## PAGE 00171: (2026x) System requirements for versions 19.0 SP3 and 19.0 SP4

- page_id: `272740508`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740508/2026x+System+requirements+for+versions+19.0+SP3+and+19.0+SP4

- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) System requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### The system requirements for 19.0 SP3 and 19.0 SP4 versions

##### Modeling Tools*

| Resource | Recommended |
| --- | --- |
| CPU | Intel Core™ i5 or higher |
| RAM | 16GB |
| Disk space | 1GB or more depending on used plugins |
| Display resolution** | Full HD (1920x1080) or higher |
| Operating Systems | Any Java SE 8-compatible operating system (Windows, Windows Server, Linux (RedHat/CentOS 7), Mac OS X). |
| Java Virtual Machine (JVM) version |  |

* MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, Cameo Enterprise Architecture

** 800x600, 1280x1024 display resolutions are compatible with a laptop or a projector.

#### NOTE: Earlier versions

Earlier versions

Windows XP and earlier versions are not supported. 
Mac OS 10.6 Snow Leopard and earlier versions are not supported.

##### FLEXnet License Server

For system requirements, refer to the [License Administration Guide](https://www.nomagic.com/files/manuals/fnp_LicAdmin_11_12_1.pdf) section 8 – “Selecting a License Server Machine” and subsection of section 9 – “System Requirements for lmadmin”.

##### Teamwork Server

| Resource | Minimum | Recommended |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CPU | PentiumTM 3, 800 MHz | PentiumTM 4, 1.4 GHz or higher |
| RAM | 512 MB | 2 GB or more |
| Disk space*** | 25 GB*** | 1 TB or more*** |
| Operating Systems | Any Java SE 8-compatible operating system (Windows, Windows Server 2019, Linux (RedHat/CentOS 7), Mac OS X). |  |
| Java Virtual Machine (JVM) version |  |  |

###### For Unix platforms, XWindow server is required for starting Teamwork Server with GUI and Teamwork Server Console.

******* Use the [formula](https://www.nomagic.com/products/teamwork-server#formula) to estimate the storage required for the Teamwork Server disk space.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740502 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00172: (2026x) System requirements for Web Application Platform

- page_id: `272740502`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740502/2026x+System+requirements+for+Web+Application+Platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:16.343+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) System requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud is a web-based product and supports the latest versions of the following browsers:

- Microsoft Edge
- Chrome
- Firefox
- Safari

##### System requirements for small and medium deployments

Small and medium deployments are deployments where 5, 10, 25, or 50 connection licenses are used.

System requirements:

- 32 GB of RAM dedicatedexclusively to Web Application Platform services
- 100 GB of free space on the disk, preferably SSD
- CPU with 8 cores running at 2.1 GHz
- Windows 2016, Windows 2019, Oracle Linux 64-bit, RedHat 8/9
- Java 21 (bundled with the installer)
- For deployments on Linux, the open file limit must be no less than 20,000 files.

##### System requirements for large enterprise deployments

Large enterprise deployments are deployments where 75, 100, or unlimited connection licenses are used.

System requirements for deployments using 75 or 100 connection licenses:

- 64 GB of RAM dedicatedexclusively to Web Application Platform services
- 200 GB of free space on the disk, preferably SSD
- CPU with 16 cores running at 2.1 GHz
- Windows 2016, Windows 2019, Oracle Linux 64-bit, RedHat 8/9 .
- Java 21 (bundled with the installer)
- For deployments on Linux, the open file limit must be no less than 20,000 files.

#### NOTE: Deployments with unlimited licenses

Deployments with unlimited licenses

If you have a deployment with an unlimited license or are in doubt about what hardware to use, contact your account executive for hardware recommendations to accommodate your specific needs.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Edge</li><li>Chrome</li><li>Firefox</li><li>Safari</li></ul><h3>System requirements for small and medium deployments</h3><p>Small and medium deployments are deployments where 5, 10, 25, or 50 connection licenses are used.</p><p>System requirements:</p><ul><li><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2ab6e136-9b5e-4a2e-a281-fcf3a1a38d94">32 GB of RAM <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">dedicated </span>exclusively to Web Application Platform services</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li><li>100 GB of free space on the disk, preferably SSD</li><li><p>CPU with 8 cores running at 2.1 GHz</p></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Windows 2016, Windows 2019, Oracle Linux 64-bit, RedHat 8/9</span></li><li>Java 21 (bundled with the installer)</li><li>For deployments on Linux, the open file limit must be no less than 20,000 files.</li></ul><h3>System requirements for large enterprise deployments</h3><p>Large enterprise deployments are deployments where 75, 100, or unlimited connection licenses are used.</p><p>System requirements for deployments using 75 or 100 connection licenses:</p><ul><li><p>64 GB of RAM <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">dedicated </span>exclusively to Web Application Platform services</p></li><li>200 GB of free space on the disk, preferably SSD</li><li>CPU with 16 cores running on Linux, the open file limit must be no less than 20,000 files.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7fbef871-442a-47af-aefa-9274794d74f7"><ac:parameter
