<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740525 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00173: (2026x) Troubleshooting

- page_id: `272740525`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740525/2026x+Troubleshooting
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:18.236+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: A script is intended for CentOS and RedHat

INLINE

###### JRE libraries are missing or not compatible

Error message while installing 32-bit *lmadmin* on 64-bit multi-arch Linux system.

The error message is misleading, the solution to fix it, is to install the 32-bit libraries.

In**Redhat/CentOS/Fedora**:

```text

```

In **recent Ubuntu** use:

```text

```

In**older Ubuntu** use:

```text

```

###### No such file or directory running *lmadmin*

That may mean that you are missing the correct Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.

Also, it may mean that you are using a 64-bit executable in a 32-bit system and vice versa.

Install the Linux Standard Base (LSB) libraries to fix it.

**libssl.so.1.0.0 library required running *lmadmin***

*lmadmin* 11.17.0 comes with libssl.so.* in its installation directory. 
You need to add these libraries in *lmadmin* directory to the LD_LIBRARY_PATH. 
 
e.g. if your *lmadmin* installation is at /opt/FNPLicenseServerManager then adding it to the path would look like this:

```text

```

###### *lmadmin* won't start

If you install *lmadmin* on a Linux-based system when logged on as root, you may encounter various issues. For example:

- When you try to launch the Web server ( http://localhost:port ) on the license server, you encounter an "Unable to connect" error.
- When you attempt to run the license server, you may encounter the following errors:

In Web.log:

- A process listing for lmadmin ('ps aux | grep lmadmin ') returns no results.
- A process listing for cameo ('ps aux|grep cameo') returns results if run right after lmadmin is started but then fails after a few seconds. If you view the cameo.log file in the logs directory (the default location is /opt/FNPLicenseServerManager/logs), you see that the process failed with the following error messages:

Therefore, when you are installing *lmadmin* on a Linux-based system, it is recommended that you avoid installing as root. If you have already installed *lmadmin* when logged on as root and you do not want to reinstall *lmadmin*, change the ownership of all of the files in the installed folder from root to another user

###### 'Can't make directory' error running *lmgrd*

Can't make directory /usr/tmp/.flexlm, errno: 2(No such file or directory).

This means that directory /usr/tmp used by *lmgrd* is missing on your system (usually on Ubuntu systems). This error does not impact license checkout.

The solution to fix it is to create a symbolic link /usr/tmp pointing to /tmp:

```text

```

###### You have no permission to install *lmadmin* in that directory

Make sure the *lmadmin* installer has read/write access.

```text

```

or

```text

```

to give read/write privileges to all files within a folder.

Then run*lmadmin*using sudo:

```text
]]>
```

###### A sample script that performs the automated installation of *lmadmin 11.14 from [nomagic.com](http://nomagic.com)* in case you need it for reference purposes

```text
/dev/null || groupadd -r lmadmin
sudo getent passwd lmadmin >/dev/null || useradd -d /home/lmadmin -g lmadmin -m -r lmadmin
sudo yum install -y ld-linux.so.2
LSB=$(yum provides /lib/ld-lsb.so.3 | grep lsb-core | tail -1 | cut -f 1 -d ' ')
sudo yum install -y $LSB
sudo echo "lmadmin ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL " >> /etc/sudoers
# If Web GUI to Flex licensing is not a must - lmgrd can be used, can be placed in rc.local to startup on boot
# usage - ./lmgrd -c PATH_TO_KEY_FILE -l PATH_TO_LOG_FILE
# RW rights needed to both files
echo "==========================================================="
echo "Getting Linux 32-bit IPv6 version 11.14 from AWS FrontCloud"
echo "==========================================================="
wget http://d1g91r27pzl568.cloudfront.net/Cameo_daemon/FlexNet_11_14/ipv6/linux/lnx_32/cameo
chmod +x cameo
echo "========================================"
echo "Getting Linux 32-bit lmgrd version 11.14"
echo "========================================"
wget https://d1oqhepk9od1tu.cloudfront.net/Flex_License_Server_Utilities/v11.14/linux32/lmgrd
chmod +x lmgrd
echo "======================================"
echo "Making flex log file named FlexLog.log"
echo "======================================"
touch FlexLog.log
chmod 664 FlexLog.log
echo "=========================================="
echo "Getting Linux 32-bit lmadmin version 11.14"
echo "=========================================="
wget https://d1oqhepk9od1tu.cloudfront.net/Flex_License_Server_Utilities/v11.14/linux32/lmadmin-i86_lsb-11_14_0_0.bin
chmod +x lmadmin-i86_lsb-11_14_0_0.bin
echo "========================================="
echo "Executing lmadmin version 11.14 installer"
echo "IMPORTANT: Install into directory /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager"
echo ""
echo " Note:  Accept all defaults for script to work properly!!!"
read -p -"Press any key to continue ...: " -n1 -s
echo "=========================================="
sudo ./lmadmin-i86_lsb-11_14_0_0.bin
sudo mkdir -p /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/licenses/cameo/
sudo cp cameo /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/cameo
sudo mv cameo /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/licenses/cameo/cameo
sudo mv lmgrd /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/lmgrd
sudo mv FlexLog.log /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/FlexLog.log
sudo chown -R lmadmin:lmadmin /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/
sudo chmod +x /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/lib*
sudo cp /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/lib* /usr/lib/
echo "======================"
echo "Opening firewall ports"
echo "======================"
FWZONE=$(sudo firewall-cmd --get-default-zone)
cat <

    lmadmin
    lmadmin
    
    

EOF
sleep 5
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=public --remove-port=8090/tcp --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=public --remove-port=1101/tcp --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=public --remove-port=27000-27009/tcp --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=internal --remove-port=8090/tcp --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=internal --remove-port=1101/tcp --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=internal --remove-port=27000-27009/tcp --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=$FWZONE --add-service=lmadmin --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --reload
IP_ADDRESS=$(ip route get 1 | awk '{print $NF;exit}')
HOSTNAME=$(hostname)
echo "$IP_ADDRESS     $HOSTNAME" >> /etc/hosts 
echo "=========================================="
echo "Creating systemd service - lmadmin"
echo "=========================================="
sudo echo "[Unit]" > /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "Description=Flexnet License Daemon" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "After=network.target network.service" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "[Service]" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "User=lmadmin" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "WorkingDirectory=/opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "ExecStart=/opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/lmadmin -allowStopServer yes" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "Restart=always" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "RestartSec=30" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "Type=forking" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "[Install]" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "WantedBy=multi-user.target" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo chown root:root /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo chmod 755 /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable lmadmin.service
echo "=========================================="
echo "lmadmin service installation complete"
echo "  usage: systemctl start|stop lmadmin"
echo "=========================================="]]>
```

If you run into any further problems with installation, please try:

- checking the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Frequently Asked Questions' space='FAQ'] for the known problems
- contacting [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support' space='SUP']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57933515-7d80-4a3d-80df-3fd9268a054f"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h4>JRE libraries are missing or not compatible</h4><p>Error message while installing 32-bit <em>lmadmin</em> on 64-bit multi-arch Linux system.</p><p>The error message is misleading, the solution to fix it, is to install the 32-bit libraries.</p><p><br /></p><p><span>In<strong> Redhat/CentOS/Fedora</strong>:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45c30515-e0b3-459c-ba24-66533cf2f3d9"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo yum install redhat-lsb-core.i686]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br />In <strong>recent Ubuntu</strong> use:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d65a4070-6035-4fbd-bdc2-799969937a9e"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo apt-get install libc6-i386
apt-get update
sudo apt-get install lsb-core]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span><br />In<strong> older Ubuntu</strong> use:</span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac06d0e2-43b0-4207-90d0-f2d725421581"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo dpkg --add-architecture i386
apt-get update
sudo apt-get install libc6:i386 libncurses5:i386 libstdc++6:i386
sudo apt-get install lsb-core]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>No such file or directory running <em>lmadmin</em></h4><p>That may mean that you are missing the correct Linux Standard Base (LSB) library.</p><p>Also, it may mean that you are using a 64-bit executable in a 32-bit system and vice versa.</p><p>Install the Linux Standard Base (LSB) libraries to fix it.</p><p><strong>libssl.so.1.0.0 library required running <em>lmadmin</em></strong></p><p><em>lmadmin</em> 11.17.0 comes with libssl.so.* in its installation directory.<br />You need to add these libraries in <em>lmadmin</em> directory to the LD_LIBRARY_PATH.<br /><br />e.g. if your <em>lmadmin</em> installation is at /opt/FNPLicenseServerManager then adding it to the path would look like this:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65e21278-e2dd-4f17-9c7b-ab39a33d4894"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$LD_LIBRARY_PATH:/opt/FNPLicenseServerManager]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><em>lmadmin</em> won't start</h4><p>If you install <em>lmadmin</em> on a Linux-based system when logged on as root, you may encounter various issues. For example:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c32620dc-9618-461f-a090-ddd729c3c331"> When you try to launch the Web server (<a href="http://localhostport">http://localhost:port</a>) on the license server, you encounter an &quot;Unable to connect&quot; error.</li><li data-uuid="afff3b6f-dbde-46e0-8859-0cc858d18496"> When you attempt to run the license server, you may encounter the following errors:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="noformat" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78f19d8a-41cb-44da-876e-5dfa1966192e"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[  10:43:19 (cameo) Vendor daemon can't talk to lmgrd (Cannot read data from license server system. (-16,287))
  10:43:20 (cameo) EXITING DUE TO SIGNAL 37 Exit reason 5]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>In Web.log:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="noformat" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7e47fd3-a0fb-4579-93fb-244019d0427a"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[08:08:08 2018] [warn] pid file /opt/FNPLicenseServerManager/logs/lmadmin.pid overwritten -- Unclean shutdown of previous Apache run?
[08:08:08 2018] [alert] getpwuid: couldn't determine user name from uid 4294967295, you probably need to modify the User directive]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li data-uuid="82b6e2b6-fa23-4140-b559-ccaa040df062"> A process listing for <em>lmadmin</em> ('ps aux | grep <em>lmadmin</em>') returns no results.</li><li data-uuid="dcbb29b2-cb37-4054-90be-672345081ba2"> A process listing for cameo ('ps aux|grep cameo') returns results if run right after <em>lmadmin</em> is started but then fails after a few seconds. If you view the cameo.log file in the logs directory (the default location is /opt/FNPLicenseServerManager/logs), you see that the process failed with the following error messages:</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="noformat" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17b95a8e-e95c-41b8-9c73-455fb951b1b0"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ 07:55:17 (cameo) Report log started (cameo/report.log).
 07:55:27 (cameo) Vendor daemon can't talk to lmgrd (Cannot read data from license server system. (-16,287))
 07:55:27 (cameo) EXITING DUE TO SIGNAL 37 Exit reason 5]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Therefore, when you are installing <em>lmadmin</em> on a Linux-based system, it is recommended that you avoid installing as root. If you have already installed <em>lmadmin</em> when logged on as root and you do not want to reinstall <em>lmadmin</em>, change the ownership of all of the files in the installed folder from root to another user</p><h4>'Can't make directory' error running <em>lmgrd</em></h4><p>Can't make directory /usr/tmp/.flexlm, errno: 2(No such file or directory).</p><p><br /></p><p>This means that directory /usr/tmp used by <em>lmgrd</em> is missing on your system (usually on Ubuntu systems). This error does not impact license checkout.</p><p>The solution to fix it is to create a symbolic link /usr/tmp pointing to /tmp:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c970dd5-f7c8-46bf-a998-802a276095f0"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ln -s /tmp /usr/tmp]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>You have no permission to install <em>lmadmin</em> in that directory</h4><p>Make sure the <em>lmadmin</em> installer has read/write access.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd829450-528a-404d-8a0d-864e72054ce1"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[chmod 755 lmadmin]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>or</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f0d610a-8a84-402a-88c4-3ee143d05f5e"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[chmod -R 755 folder_containing_lmadmin]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>to give read/write privileges to all files within a folder.</span></p><p><span> </span></p><p><span>Then run </span><em>lmadmin</em><span> using sudo:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9338b1e6-6883-4384-a59b-04a700114ac5"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo ./<lmadmin_installer>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>A sample script that performs the automated installation of <em>lmadmin 11.14 from <a href="http://nomagic.com">nomagic.com</a></em> in case you need it for reference purposes</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab322a06-fc5c-4e12-9d7f-cabbbdfbcff9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">A script is intended for CentOS and RedHat</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash
echo "==============="
echo "Installing wget"
echo "==============="
sudo yum install -y wget
echo "=================="
echo "Installing lmadmin"
echo "=================="
sudo getent group lmadmin >/dev/null || groupadd -r lmadmin
sudo getent passwd lmadmin >/dev/null || useradd -d /home/lmadmin -g lmadmin -m -r lmadmin
sudo yum install -y ld-linux.so.2
LSB=$(yum provides /lib/ld-lsb.so.3 | grep lsb-core | tail -1 | cut -f 1 -d ' ')
sudo yum install -y $LSB
sudo echo "lmadmin ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL " >> /etc/sudoers
# If Web GUI to Flex licensing is not a must - lmgrd can be used, can be placed in rc.local to startup on boot
# usage - ./lmgrd -c PATH_TO_KEY_FILE -l PATH_TO_LOG_FILE
# RW rights needed to both files
echo "==========================================================="
echo "Getting Linux 32-bit IPv6 version 11.14 from AWS FrontCloud"
echo "==========================================================="
wget http://d1g91r27pzl568.cloudfront.net/Cameo_daemon/FlexNet_11_14/ipv6/linux/lnx_32/cameo
chmod +x cameo
echo "========================================"
echo "Getting Linux 32-bit lmgrd version 11.14"
echo "========================================"
wget https://d1oqhepk9od1tu.cloudfront.net/Flex_License_Server_Utilities/v11.14/linux32/lmgrd
chmod +x lmgrd
echo "======================================"
echo "Making flex log file named FlexLog.log"
echo "======================================"
touch FlexLog.log
chmod 664 FlexLog.log
echo "=========================================="
echo "Getting Linux 32-bit lmadmin version 11.14"
echo "=========================================="
wget https://d1oqhepk9od1tu.cloudfront.net/Flex_License_Server_Utilities/v11.14/linux32/lmadmin-i86_lsb-11_14_0_0.bin
chmod +x lmadmin-i86_lsb-11_14_0_0.bin
echo "========================================="
echo "Executing lmadmin version 11.14 installer"
echo "IMPORTANT: Install into directory /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager"
echo ""
echo " Note:  Accept all defaults for script to work properly!!!"
read -p -"Press any key to continue ...: " -n1 -s
echo "=========================================="
sudo ./lmadmin-i86_lsb-11_14_0_0.bin
sudo mkdir -p /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/licenses/cameo/
sudo cp cameo /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/cameo
sudo mv cameo /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/licenses/cameo/cameo
sudo mv lmgrd /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/lmgrd
sudo mv FlexLog.log /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/FlexLog.log
sudo chown -R lmadmin:lmadmin /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/
sudo chmod +x /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/lib*
sudo cp /opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/lib* /usr/lib/
echo "======================"
echo "Opening firewall ports"
echo "======================"
FWZONE=$(sudo firewall-cmd --get-default-zone)
cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/firewalld/services/lmadmin.xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<service version="1.0">
    <short>lmadmin</short>
    <description>lmadmin</description>
    <port port="8090" protocol="tcp"/>
    <port port="1101" protocol="tcp"/>
</service>
EOF
sleep 5
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=public --remove-port=8090/tcp --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=public --remove-port=1101/tcp --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=public --remove-port=27000-27009/tcp --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=internal --remove-port=8090/tcp --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=internal --remove-port=1101/tcp --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=internal --remove-port=27000-27009/tcp --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --zone=$FWZONE --add-service=lmadmin --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --reload
IP_ADDRESS=$(ip route get 1 | awk '{print $NF;exit}')
HOSTNAME=$(hostname)
echo "$IP_ADDRESS     $HOSTNAME" >> /etc/hosts 
echo "=========================================="
echo "Creating systemd service - lmadmin"
echo "=========================================="
sudo echo "[Unit]" > /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "Description=Flexnet License Daemon" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "After=network.target network.service" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "[Service]" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "User=lmadmin" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "WorkingDirectory=/opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "ExecStart=/opt/local/FNPLicenseServerManager/lmadmin -allowStopServer yes" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "Restart=always" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "RestartSec=30" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "Type=forking" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "[Install]" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "WantedBy=multi-user.target" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo echo "" >> /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo chown root:root /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo chmod 755 /etc/systemd/system/lmadmin.service
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable lmadmin.service
echo "=========================================="
echo "lmadmin service installation complete"
echo "  usage: systemctl start|stop lmadmin"
echo "=========================================="]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If you run into any further problems with installation, please try:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="9d35c8fc-2c8a-4b98-a932-560ea5d90820"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">checking the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="FAQ" ri:content-title="Frequently Asked Questions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[FAQ section]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">for the known problems</span></span></li><li data-uuid="664fb23a-ddac-4bf6-a4bd-710bb4247117"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">contacting <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SUP" ri:content-title="Support" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customer support]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740506 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00174: (2026x) Uninstalling FlexNet license server

- page_id: `272740506`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740506/2026x+Uninstalling+FlexNet+license+server
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:16.879+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

476790241

476790253

476790243

INLINE

To uninstall the FlexNet license server

1. Go to the FlexNet Server Publisher web interface. Use the default address ([http://localhost:8090](http://localhost:8090/)) or your server name ([http://](http://docs.nomagic.com/)<server name>:8090).
2. Stop the server process:
  - Click the **Stop Server** button in the Administration section to stop the license manager and the vendor daemon.
  - End the lmadmin task process in:
    - Task Manager on Windows
    - Activity Monitor on OS X
    - System Monitor on Linux
3. Uninstall the server with an uninstaller specific to your operating system.

906118869

**For more information about uninstalling the FlexNet license server, see:**

- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.19.0.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.18.2.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_17_0.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.16.4.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_12_1.pdf']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740531 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00175: (2026x) Uninstalling modeling tools and plugins

- page_id: `272740531`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740531/2026x+Uninstalling+modeling+tools+and+plugins
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:18.554+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins installation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

##### Uninstalling modeling tools

**Uninstall on Windows**

- In the <modeling tool> installation > UninstallerData folder, double-click the Uninstall <modeling tool> file.

**Uninstall on OS X**

- Right-click the <modeling tool> installation folder and from the shortcut menu, select Move to Trash .

**Uninstall on Unix**

- In the <modeling tool> installation > UninstallerData folder, double-click the Uninstall <modeling tool> file.

##### Removing no-install package

**Windows**

- Delete the <modeling tool> installation folder.

**OS X**

- Right-click the <modeling tool> installation folder, and then, from the shortcut menu, select Move to Trash .

**Unix**

- Delete the <modeling tool> installation folder.

##### Uninstalling plugins

To uninstall plugins

1. Start the modeling tool.
2. In the Help menu, select Resource/Plugin Manager .
3. In the Resource/Plugin Manager dialog, click to select the plugin.
4. Click the Remove button.
5. When you receive the message asking if you want to remove the plugin, click OK .
6. Restart the modeling tool.

## PAGE 00176: (2026x) Updating modeling tools and plugins

- page_id: `272740533`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740533/2026x+Updating+modeling+tools+and+plugins

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To update your modeling tool and plugin, first, uninstall the earlier version, then install a later version you have obtained from the 3DS software download page.

- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing > (2026x) FlexNet license server installation and licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

- Find out the shared object dependencies in Linux. The result can show what files are missing libraries to run that file.

```text
]]>
```

- File information in Linux

```text
]]>
```

- Launching lmgrd manually via command line. lmgrd , cameo, and license file must be in the same directory:

```text
-l ]]>
```

*license_file_name*- the path to the license file. Can use only the license file name if all files are in the same directory.

*logfile_name* - the path to the log file. Can use only the license file name if all files are in the same directory. Make sure the files have read/write privileges.

- Checking server status:

```text
@]]>
```

*server_port* - the port the server is operating on (Not the cameo daemon port)

*server_host_or_ip_address* - the host/address the server is hosted on

Prior to using the command, locate*lmutil*for a particular OS in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Downloading installation files' space='']**<*product folder*****>\****Flex_Utilities_User_Guides_Vendor_Daemons\<*FlexNet version*>\Server utilities**folder.Navigate to the file via the command line and then execute the command above.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272740416 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00178: (2026x) Using modeling tools bundle

- page_id: `272740416`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/272740416/2026x+Using+modeling+tools+bundle
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:55:12.798+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > (2026x) Modeling tools and plugins installation > (2026x) Modeling tools bundle
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Using a modeling tools bundle (instructions for an employee)

To use the modeling tools bundle

1. Save the modeling tools bundle on your computer.
2. Extract the bundle.
3. To start the modeling tool, in the <modeling tool installation folder>/bin folder, double-click the <modeling_tool_name>.exe . The modeling tool is started.
4. It is recommended that users create a shortcut to the modeling tool on their desktop or a location of their choice.
5. Point to the license key file provided by the administrator or connect to the floating license server (modeling tool application > Help main menu > License Manager ). You are ready to use the modeling tool.

- For more information about modeling tools licensing, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing' space=''] page.
- Because the modeling tools bundle is not installed on an employee computer but only shared on it, there will be no application icon on the desktop or in the main menu to open the modeling tool. You can add these items manually.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The modeling tool is started.</span></li><li>It is recommended that users create a shortcut to the modeling tool on their desktop or a location of their choice.</li><li>Point to the license key file provided by the administrator or connect to the floating license server (modeling tool application &gt; <strong>Help </strong>main menu &gt; <strong>License Manager</strong>). You are ready to use the modeling tool.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d339295-1347-408f-9af6-07b5cdb0d1b1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>For more information about modeling tools licensing, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling tools and plugins licensing" /></ac:link> page.</li><li>Because the modeling tools bundle is not installed on an employee computer but only shared on it, there will be no application icon on the desktop or in the main menu to open the modeling tool. You can add these items manually.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

- page_id: `254414286`
- space_key: `IL`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2021x Refresh1 HF1 release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads.

volumes

| CATIA Magic portfolio | No Magic portfolio | CATIA Magic Academic portfolio |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

##### How to download your software

To access your download page, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

Navigate to CATIA, No Magic, release **NoMagic 2021x** select level **NoMagic 2021x****Refresh1** and select the "Fixes for this Level" **No Magic 2021x Refresh1 HF1**[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2021x Refresh2 HF1 release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads.

volumes

| CATIA Magic portfolio | No Magic portfolio | CATIA Magic Academic portfolio |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

##### How to download your software

To access your download page, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

Navigate to CATIA, No Magic, release **NoMagic 2021x** select level **NoMagic 2021x****Refresh2** and select the "Fixes for this Level" **No Magic 2021x Refresh2 HF1**

- page_id: `254414260`
- space_key: `IL`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2021x Refresh2 HF5 release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads.

volumes

| CATIA Magic portfolio | No Magic portfolio | CATIA Magic Academic portfolio |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

##### How to download your software

For instructions on how to access your download page, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

Navigate to CATIA, No Magic, release **NoMagic 2021x,** select level **NoMagic 2021x****Refresh2,** and select the "Fixes for this Level" **No Magic 2021x Refresh2 HF5**.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads. For software download instructions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

## PAGE 00184: 3DS Media list for 2022x Refresh1 release

- page_id: `254414258`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/254414258/3DS+Media+list+for+2022x+Refresh1+release

- ancestors: Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > Modeling tools and plugins installation > Downloading installation files
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2022x Refresh1 release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads. For software download instructions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

volumes

| CATIA Magic portfolio | No Magic portfolio | CATIA Magic Academic portfolio |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

## PAGE 00185: 3DS Media list for 2022x Refresh2 (and HotFix 1) release

- page_id: `254414257`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/254414257/3DS+Media+list+for+2022x+Refresh2+and+HotFix+1+release
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:30:38.128+02:00`
- ancestors: Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > Modeling tools and plugins installation > Downloading installation files
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2022x Refresh2 release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads. For software download instructions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

volumes

| CATIA Magic portfolio | No Magic portfolio | CATIA Magic Academic portfolio |
| --- | --- | --- |

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2022x release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads. For software download instructions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

volumes

| CATIA Magic portfolio | No Magic portfolio | CATIA Magic Academic portfolio |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

##### How to download your software

To access your download page, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

##### CATIA Magic portfolio media volume content

All plugins provided by us, both commercial and no-cost, are pre-bundled together with the installation files and are available to install from the **Resource/Plugin Manager**. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing plugins' space='IL'] page.

- space_key: `IL`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2024x Hot Fix 3 release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads. For software download instructions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

volumes

| CATIA Magic portfolio | No Magic portfolio | CATIA Magic Academic portfolio |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2024x Refresh1 Hot Fix 1 release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads. For software download instructions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

## PAGE 00189: 3DS Media list for 2024x Refresh1 release

- page_id: `254414253`
- space_key: `IL`

- ancestors: Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > Modeling tools and plugins installation > Downloading installation files

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2024x Refresh1 release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads. For software download instructions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

volumes

| CATIA Magic portfolio | No Magic portfolio | CATIA Magic Academic portfolio |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

##### CATIA Magic portfolio media volume content

All plugins provided by us, both commercial and no-cost, are pre-bundled together with the installation files and are available to install from the **Resource/Plugin Manager**. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing plugins' space='IL'] page.

- page_id: `254414252`
- space_key: `IL`

- ancestors: Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > Modeling tools and plugins installation > Downloading installation files

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2024x Refresh2 release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads. For software download instructions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

volumes

| CATIA Magic portfolio | No Magic portfolio | CATIA Magic Academic portfolio |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2024x Refresh3 Hot Fix 1 release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads. For software download instructions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space=''].

volumes

| CATIA Magic portfolio | No Magic portfolio | CATIA Magic Academic portfolio |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2024x Refresh3 release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads. For software download instructions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space=''].

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2024x release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads. For software download instructions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'].

## PAGE 00194: 3DS Media list for 2026x release

- page_id: `275415253`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/275415253/3DS+Media+list+for+2026x+release

- ancestors: Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > Modeling tools and plugins installation > Downloading installation files
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter contains a detailed list of all the contents of the installation bundles for the version 2026x release, along with respective file locations.

This chapter does not contain links to software downloads. For software download instructions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space=''].

volumes

| CATIA Magic portfolio | No Magic portfolio | CATIA Magic Academic portfolio |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | IMPORTANT. All Academic products are now available in the same media, CATIA No Magic Systems of Systems Architect. |

##### CATIA Magic portfolio media volume content

All plugins provided by us, both commercial and no-cost, are pre-bundled together with the installation files and are available to install from the **Resource/Plugin Manager**. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing plugins' space=''] page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254414420 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00200: Adding a license to the FlexNet server

- page_id: `254414420`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/254414420/Adding+a+license+to+the+FlexNet+server
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-06-27T13:58:37.189+02:00`
- ancestors: Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > Modeling tools and plugins licensing > FlexNet license server installation and licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

451351663

451351675

451351665

INLINE

##### Prerequisites

- The FlexNet license server is installed.
- You have the license key.

##### Procedure

1. Start the FlexNet license server.
2. Import the license key to the FlexNet license server.

To import the license key to the FlexNet license server:

1. Connect to the FlexNet License Server management Web interface. Use the default address ([http://localhost:8090](http://localhost:8090/)) or your server name (http://<server name>:8090).
2. Click Administration at the top-right corner of the webpage. When prompted to sign in, enter the default administrator's credentials (admin/admin).
3. In the FlexNet License Administrator's webpage, click Vendor Daemon Configuration on the left.
4. Click Import License and select the received license key file.
5. Click the **Import License** button. If you want to re-import the license key file, select the **Overwrite License File on License Server** check box. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

451351662

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed' space='IL']

Use the default address (<a href="http://localhost:8090/" class="external-link" rel="nofollow">http://localhost:8090</a>) or your server name (http://&lt;server name&gt;:8090).</p></li><li>Click <strong>Administration</strong> at the top-right corner of the webpage. When prompted to sign in, enter the default administrator's credentials (admin/admin).</li><li>In the <span style="color: rgb(35,35,35);">FlexNet License Administrator's webpage, click </span><strong>Vendor Daemon Configuration</strong> on the left.</li><li>Click <strong>Import License</strong> and select the received license key file.</li><li><p>Click the <strong>Import License</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3585a248-61ae-41e7-aee2-a0603aeadeb2"><ac:rich-text-body>If you want to re-import the license key file, select the <strong>Overwrite License File on License Server</strong> check box.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:alt="Apply the license key to the FlexNet license server" ac:height="473" ac:width="495"><ri:attachment ri:filename="applying_license_key.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Adding a license to the FlexNet server" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b0a3f13-095e-4ad8-aca6-ff4fc111e9d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">451351662</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed" /></ac:link><br /><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254414373 space=IL version=3 -->
## PAGE 00201: Adding the license key

- page_id: `254414373`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/254414373/Adding+the+license+key
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-07T16:40:46.985+02:00`
- ancestors: Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > Modeling tools and plugins licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

To add the license key file

1. Open the modeling tool and do one of the following:
  1. In the main menu, click Help > License Manager . The License Manager dialog opens.
  2. In the main menu, click Help > Resource/Plugin Manager . In the Resource/Plugin Manager dialog, click the Manage Licenses button. The License Manager dialog opens.
2. In the License Manager dialog, click the Select License Key Files button. Then in the Select Key File dialog, select the license key file and click Open .
3. In the License Configuration dialog, check the license information and click OK .
4. When you receive the message to restart the modeling tool, click OK .

To check the license information at a later time, select **Help** > **About** and click the **Licensing** tab in the open dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The <strong>License Manager </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the main menu, click <strong>Help</strong> &gt; <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong>. In the <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager </strong>dialog, click the <strong>Manage Licenses</strong> button. The <strong>License Manager </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="c229b6b6-cc64-431c-bea3-8f770ae7cbf5">In the <strong>License Manager </strong>dialog, click the <strong>Select License Key Files</strong> button. Then in the <strong>Select Key File</strong> dialog, select the license key file and click <strong>Open</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="a79db820-cb6d-4b6d-94d9-08553320d486">In the <strong>License Configuration</strong> dialog, check the license information and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="f8c7b554-ab2a-4873-ab40-9cdd923015fb">When you receive the message to restart the modeling tool, click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5bcfc53-37a1-4cd8-967a-eb534d101c16"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To check the license information at a later time, select <strong>Help</strong> &gt; <strong>About</strong> and click the <strong>Licensing</strong> tab in the open dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254414355 space=IL version=3 -->
## PAGE 00202: Basic concepts

- page_id: `254414355`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/254414355/Basic+concepts
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T16:30:43.505+02:00`
- ancestors: Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > Modeling tools and plugins licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| License server type | DSLS. DSLS (Dassault Systèmes License Server) is a licensing system developed by Dassault Systèmes. >]]>FlexNet. The FlexNet Publisher license server (the floating license server) from Flexera is used for managing floating licenses. >]]> |
| License type | Seat (Nodelock). The license of a modeling tool dedicated to one working place. >]]>Floating (Shareable). Allows you to install and use the same license of a modeling tool on multiple machines. Both DSLS and FlexNet license servers are used to manage floating licenses. >]]>Managed. Allows you to obtain license keys from a highly available license server managed by Dassault Systemes (SaaS), which is not installed on your premises. >]]>Please note that the license type of a modeling tool and the plugins used with the tool must be the same. |
| License category | Commercial. Standard licenses intended for professional and business use across all products.Academic. Special licenses available for the academic community. Learn more: https://www.3ds.com/products/catia/academic-license-program.Student. Special licenses available for individual students. Learn more: https://www.3ds.com/products/catia/student-license-program.Non-Profit Research. Special licenses available for non-profit research. |
| License key | A license for the dedicated Target ID that is received after purchase. >]]> |
| HostID/TargetIDTarget ID / Host ID / Computer ID | A combination of numbers and letters (also known as the MAC Address or Physical Address), which identifies the network card of a machine. It is used to tie the license to a particular machine. The Host ID is for the FlexNet license server, and the Computer ID is for DSLS. >]]> |
| Borrowing | Using a floating license in the offline mode.If a license is used on a machine that is intermittently connected to the license server, the license can be borrowed from the server and used as an offline floating license. A license can be borrowed from a license server for the selected period of time (default 24 hours).The maximum license borrowing period is 2 months. If you would like to limit the maximum borrowing period, please contact your sales representative. |
| Configuration files | The files where your defined settings and environment options are stored.When upgrading your software, you can choose one of the following:If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool or server version, select the location and click Import.If you are installing the software for the first time and/or do not want to use earlier environment options, click Use Default. |
| vendor daemonVendor daemon | A program developed by CATIA / No Magic to implement the FlexNet license server. The vendor daemon tracks how many licenses are checked out and who has the licenses. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The license of a modeling tool dedicated to one working place. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Seat licensing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="804da3b7-bea7-4b95-bed6-1dfaa19e36ff"><strong>Floating </strong>(<em>Shareable</em>). A<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">llows you to install and use the same license of a modeling tool </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">on multiple machines. Both DSLS and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">FlexNet license servers are used to manage floating licenses. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Floating licensing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></li><li data-uuid="f8863544-ada6-4aec-9657-16f7f0ac1cbb"><strong>Managed</strong>. Allows you to obtain license keys from a highly available license server managed by Dassault Systemes (SaaS), which is not installed on your premises. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Managed licensing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e87cef1-f598-4298-8be7-29601587ab54"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please note that the license type of a modeling tool and the plugins used with the tool must be the same.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>License category</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li data-uuid="cc38092d-f93e-44b5-a8a0-fbe87e8a4919"><p><strong>Commercial.</strong> Standard licenses intended for professional and business use across all products.</p></li><li data-uuid="d316eddc-c0f8-4a07-9c18-9d1a957fab23"><p><strong>Academic.</strong> Special licenses available for the academic community. Learn more: <a class="" href="https://www.3ds.com/products/catia/academic-license-program">https://www.3ds.com/products/catia/academic-license-program</a>.</p></li><li data-uuid="982ee030-6dd5-41ed-b5f1-f9f28e964d33"><p><strong>Student.</strong> Special licenses available for individual students. Learn more: <a href="https://www.3ds.com/products/catia/student-license-program" class="">https://www.3ds.com/products/catia/student-license-program</a>.</p></li><li data-uuid="6062faf5-a204-41f6-9f39-9b2a4fced751"><p><strong>Non-Profit Research.</strong> Special licenses available for non-profit research.</p></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td>License key</td><td>A license for the dedicated Target ID that is received after purchase. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Obtaining license keys" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3aa07a4-7f59-4a6a-ab59-977ce543ccb1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">HostID/TargetID</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Target ID / Host ID / Computer ID</p></div></td><td>A combination of numbers and letters (also known as the MAC Address or Physical Address), which identifies the network card of a machine. It is used to tie the license to a particular machine. The <strong>Host ID</strong> is for the FlexNet license server, and the <strong>Computer ID</strong> is for DSLS. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Determine the Target ID" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr class=""><td>Borrowing</td><td>Using a floating license in the offline mode.<br /><p>If a license is used on a machine that is intermittently connected to the license server, the license can be borrowed from the server and used as an offline floating license. A license can be borrowed from a license server for the selected period of time (default 24 hours).</p><p>The maximum license borrowing period is 2 months. If you would like to limit the maximum borrowing period, please contact your sales representative.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td>Configuration files</td><td><p>The files where your defined settings and environment options are stored.</p><p>When upgrading your software, you can choose one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c133b967-23a6-4c65-a2b1-1f065488e4d6">If you want to import the environment options from an earlier modeling tool or server version, select the location and click <strong>Import</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="d36d1154-63ac-48cd-927c-0646ee9bfbc2">If you are installing the software for the first time and/or do not want to use earlier environment options, click <strong>Use Default</strong>.</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ec9382d-0649-407a-a32d-8e6f5aa045d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">vendor daemon</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Vendor daemon</p></div></td><td><p>A program developed by CATIA / No Magic to implement the FlexNet license server. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254414419 space=IL version=2 -->
## PAGE 00203: Checking if the FlexNet server is running

- page_id: `254414419`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/254414419/Checking+if+the+FlexNet+server+is+running
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-07T17:20:17.733+02:00`
- ancestors: Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > Modeling tools and plugins licensing > FlexNet license server installation and licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

There are three ways to check if the FlexNet server is running:

1. Log on to the FlexNet License Server management Web interface. The default address is http://localhost:8090 or use your server name ( http://<server name>:8090 ). If connection is established, the FlexNet server is running.
2. Check the lmadmin task status in: If the status is Running , the FlexNet server is running.
  - Task Manager on Windows
  - Activity Monitor on OS X
  - System Monitor on Linux
3. Open the *lmadmin.log* file which can be found in *<lmadmin installation directory>\logs*. Lines in the following example identify that *lmadmin* is successfully started and the FlexNet server is running: 2015-12-09 20:28:31,781 root.LicenseEngine started without redundancy. 2015-12-09 20:28:31,958 root.Vendor (cameo) started. 2015-12-09 20:28:31,778 root Version: 11.13.1.0 build 166859 (2015.06.16-10.40.53)

If the FlexNet server is not running, open the *lmadmin.log* file, found in *<lmadmin installation directory>\logs.* The installation error codes are listed there. Use the error codes to try to find a solution in the FlexNet server License Administration Guide:

- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.19.0.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.18.2.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_17_0.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.16.4.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_12_1.pdf']

If you still can't identify the server starting problem, please contact our [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support' space='SUP'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6fa9bea3-d8a8-4dad-9f9c-d6737d4091fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1a83de3b-8a96-457b-b35d-2faee43746db">There are three ways to check if the FlexNet server is running</ac:inline-comment-marker>:</span></p><ol><li>Log on  to the FlexNet License Server management Web interface. The default address is <a href="http://localhost:8090/">http://localhost:8090</a> or use your server name (<em>http://&lt;server name&gt;:8090</em>). If connection is established, the FlexNet server is running. </li><li>Check the <em>lmadmin</em> task status in:<ul><li>Task Manager on Windows</li><li>Activity Monitor on OS X</li><li>System Monitor on Linux</li></ul>If the status is <em>Running</em>, the FlexNet server is running.</li><li><p>Open the <em>lmadmin.log</em> file which can be found in <em>&lt;lmadmin installation directory&gt;\logs</em>. Lines in the following example identify that <em>lmadmin</em> is successfully started and the FlexNet server is running:</p><pre>2015-12-09 20:28:31,781 root.LicenseEngine started without redundancy.</pre><pre>2015-12-09 20:28:31,958 root.Vendor (cameo) started.</pre><pre>2015-12-09 20:28:31,778 root Version: 11.13.1.0 build 166859 (2015.06.16-10.40.53)</pre><p><br /></p></li></ol><p>If the FlexNet server is not running, open the <em>lmadmin.log</em> file, found in <em>&lt;lmadmin installation directory&gt;\logs.</em> The installation error codes are listed there. Use the error codes to try to find a solution in the FlexNet server License Administration Guide:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fnp_LicAdmin_11.19.0.pdf"><ri:page ri:content-title="FlexNet license server installation and licensing" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License Administration Guide. FlexNet Publisher 2022 R1 (11.19.0)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fnp_LicAdmin_11.18.2.pdf"><ri:page ri:content-title="FlexNet license server installation and licensing" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License Administration Guide. FlexNet Publisher 2021 R3 (11.18.2)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fnp_LicAdmin_11_17_0.pdf"><ri:page ri:content-title="FlexNet license server 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License Administration Guide. FlexNet Publisher 2020 R2 (11.17.0)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fnp_LicAdmin_11.16.4.pdf"><ri:page ri:content-title="FlexNet license server installation and licensing" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License Administration Guide. FlexNet Publisher 2019 R2 (11.16.4)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fnp_LicAdmin_11_12_1.pdf"><ri:page ri:content-title="FlexNet license server installation and licensing" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License Administration Guide. FlexNet Publisher 2014 R1 (11.12.1)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>If you still can't identify the server starting problem, please contact our <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SUP" ri:content-title="Support" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[support team]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254414336 space=IL version=5 -->
## PAGE 00204: Creating modeling tools bundle

- page_id: `254414336`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/254414336/Creating+modeling+tools+bundle
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-10-21T09:45:28.591+02:00`
- ancestors: Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > Modeling tools and plugins installation > Modeling tools bundle
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a modeling tools bundle (instructions for an Administrator)

To create a modeling tools bundle

1. Install a modeling tool:
  1. Double click <modeling_tool_name>_<version_number>win.exe or <modeling_tool_name>*<version_* number>win64.exe .
  2. In the opened dialog, select a language and click OK . The modeling tool installation wizard opens.
  3. In the first and second steps, click Next .
  4. Select the memory allocation mode and click Next .
  5. Choose the modeling tool installation folder and click Next .
  6. If needed, choose to create modeling tool icons and click Install .
  7. Do NOT check the box to launch the modeling tool, then click Done . The modeling tool is installed.
2. Change the modeling tool's configuration files location so that the configuration files would be located in the modeling tool's installation folder:
  1. Open the <modeling_tool_name>.properties file in the <modeling tool installation>/bin folder (e.g., C:/Program Files/<modeling_tool_name>/bin ).
  2. Once you open the properties file, locate the line that starts with JAVA_ARGS=
    1. Find –DLOCALCONFIG\= true and change the option value to false ; i.e., – DLOCALCONFIG\=false
    2. **Add the following parameter: -*DWINCONFIG\=false*(Do not change any of the parameters in the *JAVA_ARGS* line during this step). Example of the 2 parameters after the changes are complete: 
*JAVA_ARGS=-Xmx12000M******-DLOCALCONFIG\=false************-DWINCONFIG\=false**********…*
  3. Save the <modeling_tool_name> . properties file. The configuration files are saved in the modeling tool's installation folder.
3. Start the modeling tool and apply a license. If the person building the bundle is using a seat license, they should **NOT**activate the license at this time so that the employees can activate the seat license key on their own computers. A floating license is a more convenient solution for large companies. For more information about licensing, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modeling tools and plugins licensing' space=''] page.
4. Install the required plugins. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing plugins' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Manager' space='MT'] pages. Make sure not to close the modeling tool after this step, as additional configuration changes are needed.
5. Specify all necessary environment options ( Options > Environment Options ). For the Environment Options dialog description, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MT'] page. The most important environment options are:
  1. Security certificates to enable secure communication between the modeling tool and Teamwork Cloud. Follow the steps at the bottom of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling secure connection between client and server' space='MCS'] page.
  2. Floating License Server information .
    1. Connect to the Floating License server and check out a license. When the person building the bundle connects to the license server successfully, the server’s name and port are set in the corresponding properties of the **Environment Options** > **Floating**section.
    2. Select the check box Auto Login to the License Server (the user can reset this setting later and select the licenses manually if needed).
  3. Teamwork Cloud Server information.
    1. In the Environment Options, click Collaboration .
    2. Select the **Server Name** and check the appropriate security choices for your deployment environment. When the person building the bundle connects to Teamwork Cloud successfully, the server’s name and the user name used to connect will be set in the Environment Options. If the person building the bundle does not want their user name to be part of the bundle, they should not connect to Teamwork Cloud before finishing the bundle. Instead, just set the Environment Options as described above.
  4. Cameo Collaborator Server information.
    1. In the Environment Options, click on Cameo Collaborator .
    2. Set the Cameo Collaborator URL.
6. Close the modeling tool.
7. Open the modeling tool (this step is mandatory if any plugins were installed during Step 4).
  1. Verify that all plugins are installed.
    1. In the Main Menu, click Help > Resource/Plugin Manager . Check if all needed plugins are marked as 'Installed'.
  2. Verify Environment Options.
    1. In the Main Menu, click Options > Environment Options and check if your settings are saved.
8. Close the modeling tool.
9. **If you want the configuration directory to be back at the default location, which is under the Users directory in Windows, then the changes made in Step 2 will need to be changed back:**** For employees to get the bundle with the predefined configuration options, the configuration files should be located in the installation folder, which is generally located in the *C:\Program Files*directory. However, since the Program Files folder is often read-only on users' business computers and the users do not have the write permissions, moving the configuration directory is very common, and so the default location for storing various configuration files is *C:\Users\<your username>\AppData\Local\.<modeling_tool_name>\<version_number>.*For more information about the configuration files' location, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='MT'] page.
  1. In the <modeling tool installation>/bin folder, open the <modeling_tool_name> .properties file.
  2. Change the -DLOCALCONFIG option value to true : -DLOCALCONFIG\=true
  3. Remove -DWINCONFIG\=false
  4. Save the *<modeling_tool_name>**.properties* file. Now, once an employee starts the modeling tool, all the configuration files will be located in the *C:\Users\<your username>\AppData\Local\.<modeling_tool_name>\<version number>***directory.** Do not launch the bundle that is being built after this step. After step 9.d, if the person building the bundle launches the bundle, then the configuration directory will no longer be in the installation directory. This will cause the final bundle not to work correctly. Wait until after Step 11, create the zip file, copy the zip file to another directory, and then extract it.
10. Change the Java path if needed. The modeling tool's *installer.exe* file installs bundled Java to the modeling tool's installation directory, which will be used by default. Copying the installation folder to another computer will copy the Java VM, too. Thus, the employee will not be required to install Java manually. For this reason, we recommend using the bundled Java. 
However, if the bundle is prepared using the no installer file (*<modeling_tool_name>_<version number>_no_install.zip*), which does not contain Java, the Java path in the *properties* file is empty, so Java needs to be installed on the employee's computer separately, and its path needs to be specified manually. To check which Java version is used by the modeling tool, go to the MagicDraw **Help**menu > **About MagicDraw**> **Environment** tab. 
To check the recommended Java version for your tool, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Java version support' space=''] page. To specify the Java path:
  1. Determine the path where Java will be installed on the end user’s computer.
  2. In the <modeling tool installation>\bin folder, open the <modeling_tool_name> .properties file.
  3. Locate the line JAVA_HOME= and change the path to JAVA_HOME=<Path to the directory where Java is installed on an employee's computer>
  4. Save the <modeling_tool_name> .properties file.
11. Compress the installation folder (you can use .zip , .rar, or any other format). You have now prepared the modeling tools bundle with already installed plugins and predefined options.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254414425 space=IL version=1 -->
## PAGE 00205: Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed

- page_id: `254414425`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/254414425/Determine+the+Host+ID+or+Target+ID+of+the+machine+on+which+the+FlexNet+license+server+is+installed
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-11-14T08:06:02.932+01:00`
- ancestors: Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > Modeling tools and plugins licensing > FlexNet license server installation and licensing > Adding a license to the FlexNet server
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

If you do not have FlexNet yet, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading Reader installation files' space='IL'] containing [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='3DS Media list for Readers 2024x' space='IL'], allowing you to set up FlexNet.

You can determine the Host ID/Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed:

- using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed' space='IL'] .
- using *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed' space='IL']*.** FlexNet license server supports only one Host ID/Target ID.

###### lmadminTo determine the Host ID/Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed (using *lmadmin*)

1. Connect to the FlexNet License Server management Web interface. The default address is[http://localhost:8090](http://localhost:8090/)or use your server name (*http://<server name>:8090*).
2. Click **Administration** at the top-right corner of the webpage. When prompted to sign in, enter the default administrator's credentials (*admin*/*admin)*.
3. Copy the Host ID/Target ID displayed as Ethernet Address (as in the following figure). [ATTACHMENT filename='ethernet_address.png']

###### lmhostidTo determine the Host ID/Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed (using *lmhostid*)

The *lmhostid* is a command line utility, which prints the Host ID/Target ID that a FlexNet license server requires on any supported system. The *Lmhostid* utility is available as an executable called *lmutil*.

1. Locate lmutil for a particular OS in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL'] <*product folder*>\Flex_Utilities_User_Guides_Vendor_Daemons\<*FlexNet version*>\Server utilities folder.
2. Run lmutil with lmhostid parameter from the command line. Lmhostid displays the default Host ID/Target ID for the current platform.

For the exact commands for a specific OS refer to: License Administration Guide (Obtaining System Hostids):

- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.19.0.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.18.2.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_17_0.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11.16.4.pdf']
- [ATTACHMENT filename='fnp_LicAdmin_11_12_1.pdf']

If two or more Ethernet addresses are available on the particular machine, the first one printed by the Host ID command should be used.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
