ac:macro-id="a11c69c4-efd7-4bb0-9d1b-7aeeeb713cb5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not have FlexNet yet, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Downloading Reader installation files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[download the free Reader installation files]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> containing <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="3DS Media list for Readers 2024x" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[FlexNet utilities]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, allowing you to set up FlexNet.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can determine the Host ID/Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed:</p><ul><li>using <em><ac:link ac:anchor="lmadmin"><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed" /></ac:link></em><em>.</em></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">using <em><ac:link ac:anchor="lmhostid"><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed" /></ac:link></em>.<em><br /></em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51981834-407c-462b-8999-90dcfeb231e9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>FlexNet license server supports only one Host ID/Target ID.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76aec613-3f11-4b11-bdbb-9d094cdb51b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">lmadmin</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To determine the Host ID/Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed (using <em>lmadmin</em>)</h4><hr /><ol><li><p><span style="color: rgb(35,35,35);">Connect to the FlexNet License Server management Web interface.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c8af5c7-000c-4391-94df-92e77515cc79"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(35,35,35);">The default address is  </span><a class="external-link" href="http://localhost:8090/" rel="nofollow">http://localhost:8090</a><span style="color: rgb(35,35,35);"> or use your server name (</span><em>http://&lt;server name&gt;:8090</em><span style="color: rgb(35,35,35);">).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Administration</strong> at the top-right corner of the webpage. When prompted to sign in, enter the default administrator's credentials (<em>admin</em>/<em>admin)</em>.</p></li><li>Copy the Host ID/Target ID displayed as Ethernet Address (as in the following figure).<br /><ac:image ac:title="Ethernet Address of the machine on which the FlexNet server is installed" ac:alt="Ethernet Address of the machine on which the FlexNet server is installed" ac:height="479" ac:width="618"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ethernet_address.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Determine the Host ID or Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e0b2e96-2cc2-4157-ac97-0b3815f96c3d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">lmhostid</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To determine the Host ID/Target ID of the machine on which the FlexNet license server is installed (using <em>lmhostid</em>)</h4><p>The <em>lmhostid</em> is a command line utility, which prints the Host ID/Target ID that a FlexNet license server requires on any supported system. The <em>Lmhostid</em> utility is available as an executable called <em>lmutil</em>. </p><hr /><ol><li>Locate <em>lmutil</em> for a particular OS in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[downloaded]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <strong>&lt;<em>product folder</em>&gt;\Flex_Utilities_User_Guides_Vendor_Daemons\&lt;<em>FlexNet version</em>&gt;\Server utilities</strong> folder.</li><li>Run <em>lmutil</em> with <em>lmhostid</em> parameter from the command line. <em>Lmhostid</em> displays the default Host ID/Target ID for the current platform.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>For the exact commands for a specific OS refer to: License Administration Guide (Obtaining System Hostids):</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fnp_LicAdmin_11.19.0.pdf"><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="FlexNet license server installation and licensing" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License Administration Guide. FlexNet Publisher 2022 R1 (11.19.0)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fnp_LicAdmin_11.18.2.pdf"><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="FlexNet license server installation and licensing" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License Administration Guide. FlexNet Publisher 2021 R3 (11.18.2)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fnp_LicAdmin_11_17_0.pdf"><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="FlexNet license server 11.17.0, 11.18.2, 11.19.0" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License Administration Guide. FlexNet Publisher 2020 R2 (11.17.0)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fnp_LicAdmin_11.16.4.pdf"><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="FlexNet license server installation and licensing" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License Administration Guide. FlexNet Publisher 2019 R2 (11.16.4)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="fnp_LicAdmin_11_12_1.pdf"><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="FlexNet license server installation and licensing" /></ri:attachment><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[License Administration Guide. FlexNet Publisher 2014 R1 (11.12.1)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e88f068a-9fea-4893-be5d-2e1c255a716b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If two or more Ethernet addresses are available on the particular machine, the first one printed by the Host ID command should be used.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254414368 space=IL version=3 -->
## PAGE 00206: Determine the Target ID

- page_id: `254414368`
- space_key: `IL`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/IL/pages/254414368/Determine+the+Target+ID
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-07T16:39:27.128+02:00`
- ancestors: Installation, Licensing, and System Requirements Documentation > Modeling tools and plugins licensing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To determine the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Basic concepts' space=''] of the machine on which the modeling tool is installed

1. Start the modeling tool.
2. When the License Manager dialog opens, click Show Target ID .
3. Select one of the following tabs based on the license server you want to use:
  1. FlexNet . Click to copy the text from the Host ID box.
  2. **DSLS**. Click to copy the text from the **Computer ID** box. Please note that more than one Target ID may be shown if you have several network adapters. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To determine the <ac:link ac:anchor="HostID/TargetID"><ri:page ri:content-title="Basic concepts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Target ID]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the machine on which the modeling tool is installed</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Start the modeling tool.</li><li>When the <strong>License Manager</strong> dialog opens, click <strong>Show Target ID</strong>. </li><li>Select one of the following tabs based on the license server you want to use:<ol><li><strong>FlexNet</strong>. Click to copy the text from the <strong>Host ID</strong> box.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>DSLS</strong>. Click to copy the text from the <strong>Computer ID</strong> box.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e05f7ab2-6409-4339-bb17-a9e29e64d2a2"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Please note that more than one Target ID may be shown if you have several network adapters.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="determine_target_id.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol></li></ol>
````
